title: Have we been building blockchains wrong?
text: Have we been building blockchains wrong?. Current blockchains require participants to download and validate every transaction and state update, forcing dependence on trusted servers or powerful hardware. By combining shared block propagation with succinct proofs, lightweight devices like phones can securely verify large blocks with minimal bandwidth. This approach decouples block size from verification costs, enabling larger blocks, increased decentralization, and optional privacy without compromising security.

title: Stablecoin-Enabled Markets
text: Stablecoin-Enabled Markets. With nearly $300 billion stablecoins in circulation and significant regulatory progress through the GENIUS Act, White House Working Group on Digital Asset Markets, and the SEC's Project Crypto initiatives, the focus is shifting from payments to new markets uniquely enabled by stablecoins' 24/7 movement capabilities. Historically, new markets have driven stablecoin adoption more than payments, with crypto exchanges in Asia fueling Tether's rise as traders held USDT as a store of value, and DeFi's 2020 boom providing USDC with venues demanding digital dollars. Three promising categories include new leverage instruments requiring instantaneous funding where stablecoins enable instant collateral deposits versus multi-day traditional wire transfers, company-to-company lending where businesses holding cash in stablecoins can engage in shorter-term capital markets, and onchain liquidity provision, and global lotteries leveraging crypto's ability to accept and distribute capital worldwide with programmable proceeds.

title: State of Cloud Security
text: State of Cloud Security. Datadog's 2025 State of Cloud Security report shows progress alongside persistent gaps across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud. IMDSv2 enforcement has increased to 49% of EC2 instances, and federated authentication adoption now covers 79% of organizations. However, 59% of AWS IAM users still have access keys older than one year, and nearly one in five EC2 instances remain overprivileged. While data perimeter adoption and public access blocking have improved, insecure defaults and lingering legacy practices continue to expose organizations to risk.

title: Stripe adds stablecoin support for subscription payments
text: Stripe adds stablecoin support for subscription payments. Stripe is rolling out stablecoin-powered subscription payments, letting businesses accept recurring transactions in USDC through the Base and Polygon blockchains. The feature, currently in private preview for US firms, allows users to pre-authorize future payments via smart contracts, solving one of crypto's long-standing hurdles. With support for 400+ wallets and integration into Stripe's full billing suite, the move could help global SaaS and AI companies cut cross-border costs and speed up settlements.

title: Anthropic revenue tied to two customers as AI pricing war threatens margins
text: Anthropic revenue tied to two customers as AI pricing war threatens margins. Cursor and GitHub Copilot account for nearly a quarter of Anthropic's income. This could undercut Anthropic's premium positioning, creating immediate pressure on Anthropic's pricing strategy and potentially threatening its hard-won dominance in AI coding. The pricing disparity will force enterprise procurement teams to reconsider vendor relationships and create unavoidable pressure in contract negotiations.

title: What digital UX can learn from the physical media revival
text: What digital UX can learn from the physical media revival. UX designers can learn from physical media to make digital streaming more human and satisfying by reducing choice, adding constraints, limiting personalization, increasing customization and curation, and even offering retro emulations. While digital convenience dominates, reflecting on the tactile, deliberate, and curated experience of physical formats can improve engagement and user satisfaction without fully abandoning streaming's speed and flexibility.

title: How an ‘Entrepreneur of the Year' Brought the First Big Bust to AI Boom
text: How an ‘Entrepreneur of the Year' Brought the First Big Bust to AI Boom. Sachin Dev Duggal was ousted as CEO of $1.5 billion AI unicorn Builder.ai after investigators uncovered $249 million in fabricated sales and "round tripping" schemes. The company, which claimed AI could automate app development but relied heavily on human programmers in India, allegedly overstated revenue by 300% while Duggal transferred investor funds to personal accounts for luxury expenses.

title: Over 290K Citizens at risk: CloudSEK Uncovers Major Data Breach at Bangalore Water Supply and Sewage Board
text: Over 290K Citizens at risk: CloudSEK Uncovers Major Data Breach at Bangalore Water Supply and Sewage Board. CloudSek's digital risk monitoring platform found a forum post selling root access to the Bangalore Water Supply and Sewage Board's database. This was traced to a publicly accessible .env file with plaintext MySQL credentials and an exposed Adminer interface. The database holds over 290,000 user records, including full name, phone number, address, Aadhaar number, and email address.

title: The Founder's Guide to Effective Social Media Use
text: The Founder's Guide to Effective Social Media Use. An investor says he won't take meetings from founders who don't post on X because he needs to see how they think before deciding if they're worth his time. Without warm referrals, investors have no way to verify you're legitimate, so they check your social presence to see how you think and whether you're making progress. The strategy is to post 5-10 times daily about non-obvious insights, commenting early on industry posts to get noticed, and relaunching your product announcement every two weeks. The point isn't to go viral, but to build a consistent record showing you can ship and think clearly over time.

Advocates argue these benefits align with core Democratic values around consumer protection, antitrust, and equity.

These practices provide comfort, therapy, and connection, but also carry risks of manipulation and harm, as seen in cases linked to AI platforms. Trust in AI remains low, though use is growing among younger people who treat these interactions as real. Brands are beginning to explore similar dynamics through worldbuilding and attachment, raising questions about how far engineered intimacy can go before it undermines human agency.

title: ECS on EC2: Covering Gaps in IMDS Hardening
text: ECS on EC2: Covering Gaps in IMDS Hardening. When launching AWS ECS tasks on EC2 instances, each task, by default, has access to the Instance Metadata Service (IMDS), which can be used to access credentials of other tasks or to escalate privileges to the instance role. Using IMDSv2 with a hop limit of 1 is not effective for stopping this attack vector, as some networking modes can still access IMDS. The article includes details for blocking IMDS access in each networking mode.

title: The Case Against pgvector
text: The Case Against pgvector. While pgvector is a useful extension for bringing vector similarity search to Postgres, it's often oversimplified in online tutorials. There are still many challenges with running pgvector in production, including complex index management, query planning difficulties with filtering, and the hidden costs of real-time indexing. Existing blog posts often omit the operational realities and trade-offs involved, and for many teams, traditional vector databases are usually simpler.

The change reassured customers about legitimacy and flexibility, which led to more conversions.

title: Bitcoin's Silent IPO
text: Bitcoin's Silent IPO. Bitcoin's weak price action despite wildly successful ETFs, institutional adoption, and friendly regulation mirrors traditional IPO dynamics where early investors methodically cash out. Meta's 2012 IPO at $38/share raising $16 billion exemplifies this pattern. Shares fell 30% over the following year as early investors distributed positions without cratering the price, creating a "sideways grind that drives everyone crazy." Onchain data shows old bitcoin moving as ETFs and institutional bids finally provide liquidity that didn't exist in 2015-2019, when selling $100 million or $1 billion would crater prices, with the distribution process typically taking 6-18 months in traditional markets and possibly longer in crypto.

title: From Zero to Hundreds of Millions: Lessons in Courageous Marketing
text: From Zero to Hundreds of Millions: Lessons in Courageous Marketing. In his new book "Courageous Marketing," former Gong CMO Udi Ledergor reveals how taking calculated risks helped transform a 12-customer startup into a billion-dollar company. His counterintuitive thesis: the safest strategy is often the riskiest because blending in means death in crowded markets. Gong's success came from tactical genius - like buying 15-second regional Super Bowl ads instead of $5M national spots, purchasing small West Coast newspaper ads and amplifying them on social media, and creating a memorable visual identity with bright colors and a bulldog mascot.

title: The security paradox of local LLMs
text: The security paradox of local LLMs. Local LLMs are vulnerable to prompt injection attacks that can lead to code vulnerabilities. Researchers discovered that smaller models like gpt-oss-20b can be tricked into generating malicious code, including backdoors and immediate remote code execution, with much higher success rates compared to frontier models. These attacks exploit the weaker reasoning and alignment capabilities of local models, turning a developer's workflow into an attack chain.

title: SEC Approves In-Kind Redemptions for Crypto ETFs
text: SEC Approves In-Kind Redemptions for Crypto ETFs. The SEC has approved in‑kind creation and redemption for all spot Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs, allowing authorized participants to exchange ETF shares directly for BTC or ETH instead of cash, which will streamline liquidity management and reduce transaction costs. This marks the first major crypto‑friendly policy under new Chair Paul Atkins, who has prioritized a fit‑for‑purpose regulatory framework for digital assets. BlackRock, Fidelity, Ark Invest, and other issuers stand to benefit, having filed for this change earlier in the year. The SEC also raised position limits on options for BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust, further enhancing institutional flexibility for hedging and arbitrage.

The mnemonic exists in three versions, ranging from one to ten seconds, with the longest intended for theatrical releases of Apple Studios films. title: Fun fatigue – is formality returning in branding?
text: Fun fatigue – is formality returning in branding?. In the early 2000s, Silicon Valley's rejection of corporate formality sparked a trend of casual, childlike branding that came to symbolize innovation and trust. However, as trust declined during the pandemic and digital spaces filled with low-quality, infantilized visuals, this style lost its impact. Now, a shift is underway, with brands embracing more formal, traditional design to convey trust, heritage, and clarity amid the chaos.

title: Building unique, per-customer defenses against advanced bot threats in the AI era
text: Building unique, per-customer defenses against advanced bot threats in the AI era. During validation, 34% of the scraping requests flagged by the new detections would not have been detected by Cloudflare's existing bot score system.

title: Building Sustainable Bank-Fintech Partnerships
text: Building Sustainable Bank-Fintech Partnerships. Ongoing monitoring, shared culture and trust, and adaptability are essential to prevent regulatory, operational, or strategic breakdowns. When done right, such partnerships enable banks to innovate faster and fintechs to scale more responsibly.

text: CISA says it will release telecom security report sought by Sen. Wyden to lift hold on Plankey nomination. Wyden claims the report contains “shocking details” about telecom vulnerabilities that could have prevented the Salt Typhoon espionage campaign if released when written initially. The Senate passed legislation requiring the report's release within 30 days, while CISA provided no timeline beyond saying it needs “proper clearance.”

Behind the scenes, Square handles real-time exchange-rate calculations and payment confirmations, simplifying the checkout experience. This launch builds on 2024's Bitcoin Conversions and complements Block's bitcoin ecosystem, including Cash App's trading features, the Bitkey self-custody wallet, Proto mining tools, and Spiral funding.

title: Gold Mines & Self-Terminating Business Models
text: Gold Mines & Self-Terminating Business Models. This founder built backyard houses until he realized his fatal flaw - customers only need one house. First customers are eager early adopters, later ones need convincing. Casper discovered this by selling mattresses that lasted 10 years, Sunrun when solar got so cheap people stopped needing loans. title: Subtleties of SQLite Indexes
text: Subtleties of SQLite Indexes. This dev improved the performance of SQLite queries for his custom content feed by understanding how SQLite's query planner uses indexes. Also, the conditions in WHERE clauses for partial indexes must exactly match the corresponding query conditions.

title: You don't need quantum hardware for post-quantum security
text: You don't need quantum hardware for post-quantum security. Organizations can prepare for quantum computing threats (Q-day) using post-quantum cryptography (PQC) deployed on existing hardware without needing expensive quantum technologies like quantum key distribution (QKD) or quantum random number generators (QRNG). Being “quantum ready” means systems remain secure after quantum computers can break conventional cryptography. Beware of vendor claims that quantum hardware products are necessary for quantum-resistant security. Organizations should prioritize implementing PQC algorithms on current infrastructure rather than investing in quantum hardware solutions that are neither necessary nor sufficient for protection against quantum adversaries.

title: TIL: `satisfies` is my favorite TypeScript keyword
text: TIL: `satisfies` is my favorite TypeScript keyword. The `satisfies` keyword in TypeScript acts as a type-safe cast, making sure an assignment is assignable to a provided type while allowing TypeScript to infer a more specific type based on the value. It's different from explicit variable type annotations, as it's best when specific type enforcement and value-based type inference are needed.

text: Building Uber's Multi-Cloud Secrets Management Platform to Enhance Security. The Secrets team created scanners to perform real-time scanning and scheduled scanning to identify secrets usage in chat apps and code, as well as built APIs for automated secrets rotation from the centralized secrets vaults. The team also developed a secrets exchange system to distribute secrets to third-party vendors.

title: Creatives share tricks for taming imposter syndrome that actually work
text: Creatives share tricks for taming imposter syndrome that actually work. Imposter syndrome is common among creatives at all levels, but it can be managed through a mix of practical strategies, community support, and mindset shifts. Techniques include documenting achievements, naming your inner critic, starting projects with small actions, focusing on facts or checklists, practicing self-compassion, and remembering that everyone experiences doubt. Acknowledging these feelings and sharing strategies helps reduce their power.

title: Saying NO is not a free action in the world of software engineering
text: Saying NO is not a free action in the world of software engineering. Saying no in software engineering, while important for work-life balance, comes with psychological and social costs, making it harder than it seems. People often avoid refusing requests due to negative connotations, potential career risks, and the desire to please others. Instead of simply telling people to "just say no," we should understand the burden of refusal and create a supportive culture that encourages saying no when necessary.

title: Tether Expanding to Nine New Chains
text: Tether Expanding to Nine New Chains. ADGM-licensed institutions can now conduct regulated activities involving USDT across nearly all networks where the token circulates, forming a multi-chain foundation enhancing liquidity and interoperability for trading, settlement, and decentralized applications. The announcement coincides with Binance securing full ADGM authorization to operate Binance.com starting January 5, through three separately licensed entities mirroring traditional financial-market architecture for exchange, clearinghouse, and broker-dealer functions.

title: The American DeepSeek Project
text: The American DeepSeek Project. Meta's recent AI shortcomings have created a vacuum in the open-source AI ecosystem that has largely been filled by Chinese models. If current dynamics continue, the AI world will be split between powerful but expensive, closed-source American models and low-cost, ubiquitous but potentially compromised Chinese models. The US likely has a very small window, roughly the next two years, to counter this trend by investing $100-500 million into an open-source model that is as good as the best closed-source model.

title: Meta is hiding $30 billion in AI infrastructure debt off its balance sheet using special purpose vehicles

title: Why your boss isn't worried about AI
text: Why your boss isn't worried about AI. The public's understanding of software bugs, while helpful for regular software, is misleading when applied to AI systems. Unlike regular software, where bugs are caused by mistakes in code and can be precisely located and fixed, AI vulnerabilities come from large training datasets, making the origin of errors difficult to pinpoint or eliminate entirely. Once a bug is fixed in regular software, it won't reappear again, but this isn't the case with AI, as even after retraining, the weird behavior may still be present, but hasn't been discovered yet.

title: Kubernetes v1.33: Storage Capacity Scoring of Nodes for Dynamic Provisioning
text: Kubernetes v1.33: Storage Capacity Scoring of Nodes for Dynamic Provisioning. Kubernetes v1.33 introduces StorageCapacityScoring, an alpha feature that adds a scoring method for pod scheduling using topology-aware volume provisioning. With StorageCapacityScoring enabled, the kube-scheduler's VolumeBinding plugin can now prioritize nodes with either the most or least available storage capacity, and the VolumeCapacityPriority feature gate will be deprecated as a result.

title: Crawling a billion web pages in just over 24 hours, in 2025
text: Crawling a billion web pages in just over 24 hours, in 2025. This dev successfully crawled a billion web pages in just over 24 hours on a budget of a few hundred dollars. The crawler was designed with politeness and fault-tolerance in mind, focusing on HTML content only and adhering to robots.txt rules. The system used a cluster of optimized nodes, each with its own Redis instance, fetcher processes, and parser processes, overcoming bottlenecks like parsing speed by using modern libraries and truncating page content.

title: You should delete tests
text: You should delete tests. While automated tests are necessary for maintaining confidence when making code changes, they should be deleted when they are flaky. Tests that are flaky, too strict, too slow to run regularly, or outdated due to changed business requirements all harm developer productivity.

title: We are replacing OOP with something worse
text: We are replacing OOP with something worse. OOP is not disappearing but shifting into new domains like microservices and distributed systems. The core principle of OOP, information attenuation and separation of concerns, is now being applied at the process and network level. This shift replaces OOP with a more cumbersome and complex system characterized by microservices, Docker, and Kubernetes.

title: MacPersistenceChecker
text: MacPersistenceChecker. MacPersistenceChecker is a native macOS security tool that enumerates all persistence mechanisms across 20+ categories, including launch agents, system extensions, and BTM database entries, scoring each item 0-100 based on code signatures, LOLBins detection, and behavioral anomalies mapped to MITRE ATT&CK framework. The tool includes real-time monitoring with optional Claude AI integration that analyzes changes using full context, including risk scores and intent mismatches, plus MCP server support enabling Claude Code integration for natural language queries against persistence data. Security teams can leverage containment features to quarantine suspicious items with automatic backup and timed release.

title: Slow Query Debugging What Broke our SaaS App
text: Slow Query Debugging What Broke our SaaS App. A slow database query calculating user credit balances took down Placid.app when high-volume API requests triggered thousands of concurrent executions of an expensive query that should have been cached. Multiple workers tried to calculate the same user's balance simultaneously and the load ballooned. This caused a cascade of failures, timeouts, and blocked queries that eventually froze the system.

title: Why Romanticizing PLG is Dangerous

Despite stories of companies hitting $50M ARR with "zero salespeople," successful PLG companies still employ significant sales teams. Relying solely on self-service creates serious limitations.

title: How to make competitors look like aliens
text: How to make competitors look like aliens. The best companies avoid direct feature comparisons with competitors and instead reframe the conversation by positioning themselves as solving entirely different problems. Asana, Superhuman, and ClickUp use analogies that place competitors in narrow roles while casting themselves as broader solutions. Good messaging defines competitors in small, accurate terms and presents the company as a more expansive, transformational option. Use templates like Tool vs. Platform or Past vs. Future to build this positioning.

title: How Do You Deal with an Executive Demanding Features? Create a Hypothesis
text: How Do You Deal with an Executive Demanding Features? Create a Hypothesis. When executives demand features based on trends, designers should respond with strategic curiosity rather than compliance or refusal, using a stakeholder-as-user approach to understand motivations and context. Designers can justify their questions by estimating the likelihood that design decisions will impact user behavior, much like engineers provide development time estimates. Summarizing ideas into testable hypotheses—"If we [change feature], users will [behavior] because [rationale], achieving [goal]"—helps expose logical inconsistencies and may lead executives to reconsider poor decisions themselves.

text: Don't Build An AI Safety Movement. Building a popular AI safety movement risks undermining existing organic public support by making it appear coordinated rather than grassroots. The author argues that broad movements inevitably get captured by adjacent political causes like labor or environmental issues, diluting safety-specific goals while exposing credible organizations to guilt-by-association with more extreme protesters.

text: Fintech's future: scaled winners and emerging disruptors. To succeed, founders are urged to focus on operational leverage, product monetization, and partnerships with incumbents.

title: Replacing a cache service with a database
text: Replacing a cache service with a database. Caches provide pre-computed data at ultra-low latencies and offer fine-grained control, eviction policies, and lightweight scaling that databases cannot yet match. While partial replicas, incremental view maintenance, and advanced structures may narrow the gap, caches remain indispensable because databases are still too heavy, costly, and connection-limited for typical cache workloads.

title: Rethink Your Pricing Strategies Amid Economic Uncertainty
text: Rethink Your Pricing Strategies Amid Economic Uncertainty. More than half of customers who consider a low-price option ultimately trade up when offered clear, tiered choices that differentiate value. This underscores the effectiveness of good, better, best pricing strategies, which highlight product value and allow businesses to serve price-sensitive customers without undermining premium offerings. During periods of economic uncertainty, marketers can use this structure not just to defend margins but also to influence buyer psychology, emphasizing long-term value and giving customers a sense of control without defaulting to broad price cuts.

title: Securing Terraform State in Azure Blob Storage using Best Practices
text: Securing Terraform State in Azure Blob Storage using Best Practices. Improperly secured Terraform state files in Azure expose critical secrets like access tokens, service principal credentials, and even plaintext passwords, making them a significant breach risk if mishandled. To safeguard these files, organizations must eliminate shared keys, isolate Storage Accounts with private endpoints, use Azure AD authentication, and enforce versioning, logging, and disaster recovery protocols.

title: State of DeFAI Q2/2025
text: State of DeFAI Q2/2025. AI is now being embedded directly into existing DeFi workflows via HUD overlays, while fully autonomous, noncustodial agents like Giza's zkML-driven vault optimizers manage over $70 million in volume. Simultaneously, major DeFi protocols (BasisOS, Zerion, Vectis, and Mode) are rolling out AI roadmaps, from ML autopilots to price-predictive vaults, to boost yield and UX. Looking ahead, the convergence of traditional analytics with AI interfaces and robust, verifiable DeFAI foundations is poised to drive the next wave of value accrual as fundamentals regain favor.

title: Does AI Threaten Vertical SaaS?
text: Does AI Threaten Vertical SaaS?. The "AI will kill vertical software" prediction started with David Friedberg on the All In podcast. text: Robo-advisor Wealthfront gets a tepid Wall Street reception for its IPO. Wealthfront finally entered public markets after 17 years as a private company, debuting at a $2.1 billion valuation but seeing its stock move only modestly on day one. The robo-advisor has benefited from strong growth in high-yield cash accounts, pushing assets under management to roughly $90 billion and delivering profitability alongside 26% year-over-year revenue growth. The muted reception highlights investor caution toward fintech listings, even as Wealthfront joins peers like Chime and Klarna in testing the public markets.

title: Klarna takes on banks with its own debit card
text: Klarna takes on banks with its own debit card. Klarna has launched a debit card in the U.S., marking a major push beyond buy now, pay later into core banking services. title: The HALO Effect
text: The HALO Effect. Google paid $2.4B to "hire" Windsurf's team while the startup somehow keeps operating. These aren't acquisitions - companies hire the founders and license the IP while the startup continues under new leadership. Kevin Kwok calls them HALOs (Hire And License Out). Antitrust killed normal acquisitions, but that's not the real story. In AI, value isn't in products that go obsolete yearly - it's in teams who know how to ship at the frontier. Google doesn't want Windsurf's $80M revenue. It wants people who can transform models into products. HALOs close in days because they're honest about what matters - the people.

title: Where models' eyes should look in ads
text: Where models' eyes should look in ads. Ads are more effective when the model's eye contact matches the product type. For functional or emotionally negative products, having the model look directly at the viewer increases effectiveness, as seen in a 12% boost for a cafe ad and a 40% increase in sign-ups for an anti-smoking ad. For pleasurable products, a model looking away leads to higher engagement, such as a 30% rise in sunhat sales and a 19% improvement in watch ad ratings. Direct gaze builds trust and credibility, while averted gaze helps viewers imagine themselves using the product.

title: From Ilya's deposition
text: From Ilya's deposition. Ilya Sutskever plotted for over a year with Mira Murati to remove Sam Altman as OpenAI CEO. Dario Amondei wanted Greg Brockman fired and himself in charge of all research. Murati told Sutskever that Altman had pitted her against Daniela Amondei. Sutskever wrote a 52-page-long memo to get Altman fired and also a separate document on Brockman. OpenAI is paying Sutskever's legal bills.

title: Vertical vs. Horizontal Org Coupling
text: Vertical vs. Horizontal Org Coupling. Organizational effectiveness depends on balancing cohesion and coupling, adapting structures fluidly, and managing dependencies without assuming any state is inherently optimal.

title: Customers Expect Empathy
text: Customers Expect Empathy. Most consumers want brands to show real empathy, yet many companies fall short and lose trust and loyalty. Empathy strongly influences buying decisions and even supports higher prices, but customers doubt most brands genuinely care. This gap grows as AI takes over more interactions.

title: The nuance of React rendering behaviour as it relates to children
text: The nuance of React rendering behaviour as it relates to children. React child components behave differently when rendered directly versus passed through the `children` prop. The main difference is in object reference equality: when components are rendered directly, React creates new object references on each render, causing child components to re-render, but when passed via `children`, the same object reference is reused, preventing unnecessary re-renders.

title: Open banking on hold: what the CFPB's pause reveals about the future of financial data
text: Open banking on hold: what the CFPB's pause reveals about the future of financial data. A federal court's injunction has frozen the CFPB's open banking rule, raising fresh questions about the balance of power in US financial data. The decision does more than halt a rule - it reopens a debate that sits at the center of modern finance: who controls consumer data, and under what conditions it can move. Judge Danny C. Reeves of the US District Court for the Eastern District of Kentucky issued the injunction, pausing the agency's Personal Financial Data Rights rule and forcing a regulatory rethink. For now, banks gain breathing room, fintechs lose momentum, and consumers remain locked out of their own data, as the US decides whether open banking will empower innovation or entrench the status quo.

title: HelloFresh's Brownfield Leap: One App to Feed Them All!
text: HelloFresh's Brownfield Leap: One App to Feed Them All!. HelloFresh Group launched Project PUMA (Project Unified Mobile App) to consolidate its fragmented mobile app infrastructure, which previously used separate native Swift/Kotlin codebases for meal kit brands and React Native for ready-to-eat brands. It chose a "brownfield" React Native approach that allows progressive migration while keeping existing native apps functional, enabling them to build features once and deploy across all brands.

title: MetaMask Releases Solana Support
text: MetaMask Releases Solana Support. MetaMask's browser extension now natively supports Solana, allowing users to send, receive, swap, bridge, and purchase Solana-based tokens and interact with Solana dApps alongside their Ethereum accounts in a single interface. Solana support on the MetaMask mobile app will arrive in the coming weeks. MetaMask's security features on Solana include real-time alerts, malicious dApp detection, and transaction simulations.

title: How to Build Cohort Charts
text: How to Build Cohort Charts. Most founders can quote retention rates, but few can show them. This guide breaks down the three cohort charts every operator should know - range retention tables, spider charts, and stacked area charts - and explains when to use each. You'll learn how to build them, interpret the patterns, and turn a pile of numbers into a story about what's really happening in your customer base.

title: AWS CDK Refactor Feature: Safe Infrastructure as Code Renaming
text: AWS CDK Refactor Feature: Safe Infrastructure as Code Renaming. AWS has a new CDK refactor feature that lets engineers safely rename and reorganize infrastructure as code without triggering destructive resource replacements, reducing downtime and data loss risks.

title: Provider of covert surveillance app spills passwords for 62,000 users
text: Provider of covert surveillance app spills passwords for 62,000 users. Catwatchful, an Android spyware app marketed for "stealthy monitoring," leaked sensitive data from 62,000 users due to an SQL injection vulnerability. The breach exposed email addresses, plain-text passwords, and monitoring data.

title: JSON Structure: A JSON schema language you'll love
text: JSON Structure: A JSON schema language you'll love. JSON Structure is a strictly typed data definition language that describes JSON-encoded data so that mapping to and from programming languages and databases becomes straightforward.

title: AI in Practice Survey
text: AI in Practice Survey. The AI in Practice survey maps where adoption is happening, where the gaps are, and how teams are hardening and scaling AI in practice.

text: Prove GitHub Copilot's Real Value Beyond "Developers Seem to Like It". Hype doesn't pay the bills. Can you quantify the ROI of spending $252/seat on GitHub Copilot (or other coding assistants)? This no-buzzword webinar shows how engineering leaders can answer this question with cold hard metrics, rather than ‘trust me bro'. Learn how to go beyond usage dashboard and track productivity gains in cycle times and commit velocity, monitor code quality/security risks, and compare effectiveness across platforms like Copilot, Cursor, and Amazon Q. Watch on demand: How to Measure the True Impact of AI Code Assistants like GitHub Copilot

title: Salesloft Drift Data Breach: Update to Our Customers
text: Salesloft Drift Data Breach: Update to Our Customers. PagerDuty reported that attackers obtained its credentials from the Salesloft Drift breach and may have accessed Salesforce data, prompting the company to disable Drift's access while investigating. Customers are advised to remain vigilant against phishing and social engineering due to possible exposure of names, phone numbers, and email addresses.

title: Build Your Own Edge: Low-Code Tools for Smarter Competitive Research
text: Build Your Own Edge: Low-Code Tools for Smarter Competitive Research. Product marketers can boost their competitive research by building custom, low-code tools that automate tracking and analysis, saving time, cutting costs, and enabling tailored insights.

title: Robinhood adds advanced trading tools to retain active users
text: Robinhood adds advanced trading tools to retain active users. Robinhood Markets has upgraded its mobile app with new features, such as options trade preview simulations and enhanced real-time charting. With roughly 63% of Q1 revenue coming from customer trades, these enhancements are critical as CEO Vlad Tenev pursues a vision to transform Robinhood into a full-service global financial platform. The move aims to deepen engagement with millennials and Gen Z traders while laying the groundwork for upcoming offerings like bank accounts, credit cards, and wealth-management services.

title: Brut RB
text: Brut RB. Brut is a Ruby-based framework for building web apps with HTML, JavaScript, CSS, and database interactions. It's structured around concepts like pages, forms, handlers, and components for the front-end, and database models and domain logic for the back-end.

title: AI, networks, and Mechanical Turks
text: AI, networks, and Mechanical Turks. LLMs let companies borrow intent-level understanding instead of building it, changing how recommendation and discovery systems start, scale, and shape what people see.

title: AlphaGenome: AI for better understanding the genome
text: AlphaGenome: AI for better understanding the genome. AlphaGenome, an AI tool by DeepMind, enhances the prediction of variant effects in human DNA sequences, improving our understanding of genome function and regulation. It processes up to 1 million DNA letters and predicts diverse molecular properties, offering unparalleled precision and comprehensive insights into genetic variants. Available as a preview API, AlphaGenome aims to advance disease research, synthetic biology, and fundamental genomics.

title: How to Solopreneurship in 2025
text: How to Solopreneurship in 2025. To be a successful solopreneur in product and growth, focus on attracting high-value clients through targeted content and networking. Offer skills aligned with your passion, identify gaps, and refine expertise. Monetize by specializing, understanding your value, and respecting your time.

title: When 'perfect' code fails
text: When 'perfect' code fails. A critical security bug found in this dev's Next.js application was caused by a seemingly perfect one-line equality check. Due to Next.js's server function implementation, the synchronous function was unexpectedly converted to an asynchronous function returning a Promise. In JavaScript, a Promise evaluates to true in an `if` statement, effectively bypassing the security check and granting access to everyone.

title: Squashing my dumb bugs and why I log build IDs
text: Squashing my dumb bugs and why I log build IDs. A bug was introduced by a misplaced negation when handling user-agent headers in a C++ project. This mistake, caused by a copy-paste error while refactoring code to use "if scope" for error handling, resulted in user-agent data not being captured for a specific build. To clear up confusion for users and pinpoint the faulty data, the author added a visual "fingerprint" that represented the build's git commit hash to the reporting page, allowing users to quickly identify data affected by the buggy version.

title: The Fed Just Opened the Door to Crypto
text: The Fed Just Opened the Door to Crypto. Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller declared that crypto and distributed ledgers are now “woven into the fabric” of US finance, signaling a major policy shift toward embracing digital assets. The central bank is entering “a new era” where DeFi innovators are welcome to shape the future of payments instead of being sidelined. Waller proposed a “skinny master account”, a limited-access payment account for fintechs and crypto firms that would connect directly to Fed payment rails without full banking privileges. The move reflects the Fed's broader retreat from anti-crypto stances and could open the door for regulated blockchain-native payment systems to plug directly into US monetary infrastructure.

title: Want People to Care About Users? Lay Out What's at Stake if They Don't
text: Want People to Care About Users? Lay Out What's at Stake if They Don't. Effective UX storytelling starts by establishing clear stakes. It requires recognizing the key decision-makers and clarifying why the current design is inadequate. Additionally, it's essential to present the consequences of inaction. Vague data and generic processes can weaken the overall message. Engagement and action become more likely when findings are directly linked to business outcomes while conveying a sense of urgency.

title: Princeton University Data Breach Impacts Alumni, Students, Employees
text: Princeton University Data Breach Impacts Alumni, Students, Employees. Princeton University disclosed a data breach after a hacker accessed a database with personal details of alumni, donors, faculty, students, and parents. The attack, traced to a phishing call, compromised information like names, addresses, emails, and donation records, but not passwords or financial data.

title: The AI Guide to Capturing and Converting More Leads

Learn how the smartest businesses are using AI to fuel smarter marketing and drive more revenue. 👉 Get the guide

title: ScarCruft Uses RokRAT Malware in Operation HanKook Phantom Targeting South Korean Academics
text: ScarCruft Uses RokRAT Malware in Operation HanKook Phantom Targeting South Korean Academics. North Korean APT37 (ScarCruft) carried out Operation HanKook Phantom using spear-phishing emails with malicious LNK files disguised as PDFs to deliver RokRAT malware to South Korean research targets. The attack utilized fake newsletter lures from the National Intelligence Research Association, employing PowerShell scripts and fileless techniques to exfiltrate data via Dropbox, Google Cloud, and Yandex. This campaign shows APT37's focus on espionage against the South Korean government, research, and academic targets.

title: KServe becomes a CNCF incubating project
text: KServe becomes a CNCF incubating project. KServe, a Kubernetes-based AI inference platform used by Bloomberg, Red Hat, and NVIDIA, was accepted into the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) as an incubating project in September.

title: Security Pros Are Drowning in Threat-Intel Data and It's Making Everything More Dangerous
text: Security Pros Are Drowning in Threat-Intel Data and It's Making Everything More Dangerous. A Google Cloud and Forrester study of 1,541 tech leaders found that 61% of security professionals are overwhelmed by threat intelligence data, while 60% lack skilled analysts. Top concerns include phishing (46%), ransomware (44%), and AI prompt injection attacks (34%).

title: Call Intelligence that doesn't suck.
text: Call Intelligence that doesn't suck.. Retellio listens to every sales call, surfaces the key customer insights, and delivers them as a podcast, newsletter, or Slack message. Giving sales leaders super intelligence that is easy and fun to consume. See how it works.

title: Trade, Tariffs, and Tech
text: Trade, Tariffs, and Tech. A Chinese invasion of Taiwan would cripple global supply chains and economic output, with lasting impacts on tech and trade regardless of the outcome. While Trump's tariffs aim to reduce reliance on China and revive U.S. manufacturing, such top-down efforts are risky and likely less effective than adapting within the existing global system.

title: Voice and Immersive Interfaces: Preparing Your Product for the Future of UX
text: Voice and Immersive Interfaces: Preparing Your Product for the Future of UX. Voice and immersive interfaces are transitioning from novelty features to operational necessities, with major organizations like NHS trusts, Tesla, and Boeing already implementing them to enhance productivity and user experience. These technologies excel where traditional interfaces struggle, enabling hands-free interaction, improving accessibility for users with disabilities, and providing spatial understanding for complex tasks. Companies should audit their most common user actions for voice potential and adopt voice-first thinking, as users increasingly expect more intuitive, contextual, and human-like digital interactions.

title: Binary Formats are Better Than JSON in Browsers!
text: Binary Formats are Better Than JSON in Browsers!. JSON is now often outperformed by binary formats like Avro, Protobuf, and Bebop for data-intensive web apps. New libraries today allow for faster deserialization of binary data. Bebop, Avro, and Protobuf have advantages in speed and type safety, overcoming JSON's limitations, such as string input requirements and lack of schema validation.

title: How AI is re-humanizing shopping
text: How AI is re-humanizing shopping. Buyers crave simpler shopping experiences and are tired of hitting digital dead ends. A new report by Bloomreach reveals that: 97% of shoppers who have used an AI shopping assistant find it helpful. Over 46% believe that an AI assistant would offer more honest advice about an outfit than a friend. Conversational shopping increases revenue per visit by 33% on average. Learn how conversational AI is teaching people to shop like humans again — and what that means for your brand. 👉 Read key insights from the report or download the full version

title: Meet the new GKE: Extending Autopilot to all qualifying clusters
text: Meet the new GKE: Extending Autopilot to all qualifying clusters. GKE Autopilot now extends its fully managed, container-optimized compute platform to all qualified clusters, allowing rapid horizontal and vertical scaling, efficient resource use, and simplified cluster operations. Users can run workloads with standard or specialized hardware, set Autopilot as the default for namespaces, and adopt new automatically provisioned compute classes without disrupting existing deployments.

title: Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling now supports forced cancellation of instance refreshes
text: Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling now supports forced cancellation of instance refreshes. Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling now allows forced cancellation of instance refreshes, enabling immediate abortion of in-progress instance launches or terminations.

title: Track Weight
text: Track Weight. TrackWeight is an open-source macOS app that turns a MacBook's trackpad into a digital weighing scale by using Force Touch sensors and the Open Multi-Touch Support library.

title: FBI Wants to Know Who Runs Archive.ph
text: FBI Wants to Know Who Runs Archive.ph. The FBI issued a federal subpoena to domain registrar Tucows demanding extensive billing records, internet session logs, payment information, and cloud computing service details to unmask the anonymous operator behind archive.today (Archive.is and Archive.ph), a web archiving service operational since 2012 that's commonly used to bypass news paywalls, though the subpoena does not specify what crime is being investigated.

title: In First Big Marketing Push, OpenAI Makes ChatGPT Free for College Students
text: In First Big Marketing Push, OpenAI Makes ChatGPT Free for College Students. OpenAI launched its first major campaign by offering US and Canadian college students free access to ChatGPT Plus through May. The campaign targets students ahead of finals with ads across billboards, digital, and podcasts, highlighting use cases from studying to job hunting.

title: Bank startup Airwallex valued at $6.2Bn with new funding
text: Bank startup Airwallex valued at $6.2Bn with new funding. Airwallex has raised $200 million in fresh funding, pushing its valuation to $6.2 billion and reaffirming investor confidence in the cross-border payments startup. The round was led by existing backer Lone Pine Capital, with support from Sequoia and Tencent, signaling strong momentum despite a broader tech funding slowdown. Airwallex, which enables businesses to manage international payments and finances, now plans to accelerate expansion in the U.S. and introduce new AI-driven financial tools.

title: azurevelo
text: azurevelo. azurevelo deploys a Velociraptor container on Azure App Service to aid incident responders.

title: Attention Is the Last Scarce Resource
text: Attention Is the Last Scarce Resource. Attention is becoming the most valuable and limited resource in the digital economy. Platforms like TikTok now dominate consumer behavior, with Gen Z spending 108 minutes per day on the app, contributing to declining interest in traditional media like The Late Show. As AI-generated video and real-time tools like Mirage flood the internet with content, attention spans shrink, and the cost of capturing attention rises. To adapt, companies are expanding into new attention-friendly mediums like voice and using new business models such as subscriptions and microtransactions to extract more value per user.

title: The Wheel of Nothing
text: The Wheel of Nothing. Many websites use a rotating headline tactic—dubbed “The Wheel of Nothing”—to cycle through audience types in a bid for inclusivity, but it often feels generic and unfocused. Even top design firm Pentagram recently used this approach, though its deeper messaging shows a much clearer brand identity. The takeaway: strong branding demands focus—trying to appeal to everyone risks resonating with no one.

title: JLR: Payroll data stolen in cybercrime that shook UK economy
text: JLR: Payroll data stolen in cybercrime that shook UK economy. Jaguar Land Rover confirms that a cyberattack in August not only halted factory production, it also exposed payroll data for current and former staff, including bank details and tax information. The incident has driven reported sales losses of £1.5 billion, plus £196 million in related losses, and is estimated to have cost the broader UK economy up to £2.1 billion, with the Scattered Lapsus Hunters group blamed.

title: Apple addresses more than 100 vulnerabilities in security updates for iPhones, Macs, and iPads
text: Apple addresses more than 100 vulnerabilities in security updates for iPhones, Macs, and iPads. Apple released unusually large security updates patching 105 vulnerabilities in macOS 26.1 and 56 in iOS/iPadOS 26.1.

title: Vitalik Buterin pushes for trustless gas futures market
text: Vitalik Buterin pushes for trustless gas futures market. Vitalik Buterin has proposed a trustless onchain gas futures market that allows users to prepay for specific gas quantities in defined time intervals, but Flashbots strategist Hasu argued the market lacks a natural short side as many want to hedge gas exposure, but no one is long gas at scale. Buterin suggested the protocol itself could auction basefee claiming rights to move from long to neutral while users shift from short to neutral, though Gnosis co-founder Martin Koppelmann countered that Ethereum's burn mechanism eliminates validators as natural sellers who would otherwise hedge without significant risk premiums.

title: Ferrari Loses Logo Battle Against Bizarre Opponent
text: Ferrari Loses Logo Battle Against Bizarre Opponent. Ferrari lost a trademark dispute with Malaysian energy drink brand Wee Power over the use of horse imagery in its logos. The High Court ruled that Wee Power's design, which features two red horses facing each other with a 'W' background, didn't infringe Ferrari's prancing horse trademark. Ferrari was ordered to pay legal costs but has filed an appeal at the KL High Court.

title: Tadao Ando Brings a Fruity Minimalism to His First Watch
text: Tadao Ando Brings a Fruity Minimalism to His First Watch. Renowned Japanese architect Tadao Ando collaborated with Swiss brand Cauny on his first complete watch design, creating a minimalist timepiece inspired by a green apple.

title: TikTok's Bulletin Board
text: TikTok's Bulletin Board. TikTok launched its bulletin board feature to help brands and creators send direct updates and exclusive content to followers in a simple one-to-many format. The feature has already been used in beta. Followers can join a creator's bulletin board from the profile page and receive inbox notifications they can react to with emojis. The feature is available to creators who are at least 18 years old and have 50K followers or more.

title: Adobe Launches AI Assistants for Express and Photoshop
text: Adobe Launches AI Assistants for Express and Photoshop. Adobe launched AI assistants for Express and Photoshop to help users create and edit images more efficiently. Express features a dedicated AI mode for text-based image generation that users can toggle with traditional editing tools, while Photoshop's sidebar assistant can understand layers, automate object selection, and handle repetitive tasks like background removal. Adobe is also testing "Project Moonlight," which coordinates multiple Adobe assistants and connects to creators' social channels to understand their style.

title: Trapped in Sales Mode
text: Trapped in Sales Mode. Customer Trend Dashboards help product managers in sales-driven environments transform scattered customer feedback into actionable insights that drive product improvements without sidelining growth. They bridge the gap between sales pressure and product progress by surfacing patterns, aligning teams, and tracking momentum.

title: Open Global Investment as a Governance Model for AGI
text: Open Global Investment as a Governance Model for AGI. Rather than nationalizing AI companies or creating new international bodies, we should create publicly-traded AGI corporations with stricter government oversight. Foreign countries and citizens would purchase voting stock in companies like OpenAI, giving them financial stakes and board influence instead of racing to build competing systems.

title: Google Has Your Data. Gemini Barely Uses It
text: Google Has Your Data. Gemini Barely Uses It. Google is dramatically under-capitalizing on the strongest context position in the industry. Gemini hides its workspace connector in its settings, treating it as an optional enhancement, not the center of the product. This is almost certainly because Google is trying to play it safe. The company has an opportunity to turn its dormant context advantage into experiences that would be impossible anywhere else.

title: Bilt raises $250M at $10.75B valuation, plans card relaunch
text: Bilt raises $250M at $10.75B valuation, plans card relaunch. Bilt has closed a $250M round led by General Catalyst and GID, with a $100M strategic investment from UWM. The fintech now covers 1 in 4 U.S. apartment buildings and projects $1B+ in revenue by Q1 2026 and $100B in annual housing transactions by year-end. It is expanding into condos, HOAs, and student housing with partners like Douglas Elliman and Blackstone. A new “Bilt Card 2.0”, co-developed with Cardless, will be launched in February 2026 with three tiers ($0, $95, and $495 annual fees).

title: Aerodrome Expands Beyond Base with Ethereum Integration
text: Aerodrome Expands Beyond Base with Ethereum Integration. Aerodrome's move from Base to Ethereum isn't a loss for Base but proof of its success.

text: GenAI as a shopping assistant set to explode during Prime Day sales. GenAI is set to significantly influence Amazon's Prime Day sales, with Adobe predicting a 3,200% increase in AI-driven traffic. This four-day event expects $23.8 billion in online sales, a 28.4% year-over-year growth. GenAI tools enhance shopping experiences, aiding in research, product recommendations, and deal finding.

text: Carl Rivera - Shopify's big bet on design and craft as the differentiator. In the AI age, real advantage comes from exceptional craft, not speed. Teams win by removing abstraction, focusing on core experiences, and blending product, design, and engineering to create work that feels real, personal, and worth returning to.

text: Creating Intelligent Products. Today's most successful intelligent products combine deterministic systems with probabilistic reasoning, and product teams must learn to treat this probabilistic approach not as a novelty, but as essential to creating valuable, adaptive experiences.

title: Square outshines rivals with sleekest, cheapest handheld POS
text: Square outshines rivals with sleekest, cheapest handheld POS. Square's new handheld POS is the thinnest, lightest, and most affordable option available, outperforming Toast, Clover, and Shift4 on hardware specs and pricing.

title: Eve Raises $103 Million at $1 Billion Valuation to Help Plaintiff Firms Deliver Justice Through AI Transformation
text: Eve Raises $103 Million at $1 Billion Valuation to Help Plaintiff Firms Deliver Justice Through AI Transformation. Eve raised $103 million in Series B funding, led by Spark Capital, reaching a $1 billion valuation. The AI platform now serves over 450 law firms, processing 200,000 legal cases annually and assisting in recovering over $3.5 billion in settlements. Eve's rapidly growing legal AI platform aims to reimagine plaintiff firms' operations by enhancing efficiency and scaling case capacity.

title: Building a 60% AI Team: Efficient, Profitable, and Surprisingly Lonely
text: Building a 60% AI Team: Efficient, Profitable, and Surprisingly Lonely. AI may be a necessity in 2025 to be competitive. The unit economics alone make it almost impossible to justify hiring humans for many functions. Tutoring an AI is much easier in many cases. However, an AI team is a quiet one - it can be lonely when group meetings are gone and there's no one to grab after work drinks with.

title: Fraudster stole over $1.5 million from city of Baltimore
text: Fraudster stole over $1.5 million from city of Baltimore. A fraudster stole $1.5 million from Baltimore between February and March by pretending to be a vendor employee, hacking into the vendor's Workday account, and convincing AP staff to redirect bank details. The city recovered $721,000, but cannot recover $803,000 despite insurance claims and reissued payments. This is Baltimore's third instance of vendor fraud since 2019, highlighting ongoing weaknesses in financial controls following losses of $62,000 in 2019 and $376,000 in 2022.

title: apktool-mcp-server
text: apktool-mcp-server. apktool-mcp-server provides a fully automated MCP server built on top of apktool to analyze Android APKs using LLMs.

title: Klarna files for U.S. IPO to raise up to $1.27 billion
text: Klarna files for U.S. IPO to raise up to $1.27 billion. Klarna has officially filed to go public in the U.S., aiming to raise up to $1.27 billion in what could be one of the largest fintech IPOs of the year. The Swedish BNPL giant will list on the Nasdaq under the ticker "KLAR" and plans to use proceeds for international expansion and AI investments. The IPO follows a strong rebound in Klarna's financials, including a return to profitability and growing traction in the U.S. market, where it now serves over 40 million users.

title: How Google Cloud is securing open-source credentials at scale
text: How Google Cloud is securing open-source credentials at scale. Google Cloud has developed a scanning tool that automatically detects leaked credentials in open-source artifacts, such as packages and Docker images, helping prevent misuse and improve security across the software supply chain. The system enables near-instant remediation of credential exposures. It will soon expand to include third-party credentials and broader open-source platform coverage.

title: Building Reward Functions for Chemical AI: A Tale of Reward Hacking
text: Building Reward Functions for Chemical AI: A Tale of Reward Hacking. FutureHouse researchers detail the months-long battle against their chemistry reasoning model, ether0, which kept finding creative ways to game their reward systems—from proposing reactions that just "add water and call it a reaction" to generating explosive peroxide chains. Their iterative fixes using bloom filters and structure alerts reveal why domain expertise, not just ML engineering, has become critical for training reasoning models.

title: PAM without vaults
text: PAM without vaults. If engineer access to resources means passwords, vaults, or tickets, your PAM is slowing you down. Teleport removes access blockers by replacing vaulted credentials and ticket queues with short-lived, cryptographic identity and ephemeral authorization. No more standing privileges, access friction, or credential handling. Try Teleport for yourself.

title: Western Union Eyes Stablecoins as Remittance Moat Shrinks
text: Western Union Eyes Stablecoins as Remittance Moat Shrinks. With revenue down 25% as digital-first rivals like Wise and Remitly take share, Western Union (WU) is piloting stablecoin settlement in South America and Africa. Its edge isn't the tech - others can adopt stablecoins too - but instead its 400K global cash agent network, which could make WU the key bridge from cash to stablecoins for individuals and fintechs. This pivot could buy time against the decline of cash while positioning WU as infrastructure for the digital economy.

title: RTÉ Investigating Potential Cyber Security Incident
text: RTÉ Investigating Potential Cyber Security Incident. The UK's National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) contacted the Irish broadcaster RTÉ regarding a potential cybersecurity threat, based on information that it had received.

title: KubeCon Europe: How Kubernetes Handles 6G, LLMs, and Deep Space
text: KubeCon Europe: How Kubernetes Handles 6G, LLMs, and Deep Space. KubeCon + CloudNativeCon Europe last week highlighted how Kubernetes must evolve to support cutting-edge use cases like 5G/6G telecom and large language models, with community-driven innovation playing a key role. Keynotes emphasized the need for standardized APIs and dynamic load balancing to handle complex, resource-intensive workloads across diverse environments.

title: Finishing Our Sentences
text: Finishing Our Sentences. Speak in money, not features. Rough revenue guesstimates win executive attention and drive better product decisions.

title: Fake LinkedIn jobs trick Mac users into downloading Flexible Ferret malware

title: Leverage on Prediction Markets is Possible
text: Leverage on Prediction Markets is Possible. Leverage in prediction markets faces fundamental challenges because instant price jumps to zero eliminate liquidation windows, requiring financiers to price expected losses upfront, but naive probability-based fees merely create accounting tricks where levered ROI equals unleveraged positions. The solution splits price movements into "creep" (continuous drift allowing liquidation) versus "jumps" (instant gaps with no reaction time) and prices risk over short epochs with rolling per-epoch fees similar to perps funding rather than pricing entire position lifespans. This epoch-based approach treats drift, volatility, and jump rates as frozen constants within small time windows, computing liquidation probabilities and average losses separately for creep versus jumps, while limiting misspecification risk that could wildly over- or underestimate fees as market conditions shift near resolution dates. The architecture includes block-level auction designs capturing news-driven arbitrage to rebate market makers and financiers, further reducing effective jump losses and fees charged to traders.

title: Combined Metrics for Tracking Smart Contract Networks
text: Combined Metrics for Tracking Smart Contract Networks. Using 30-day averages and normalized market-share conditions for “size” alongside week-on-week min-max and Z-score aggregations for “growth” surfaces major shifts without requiring deep dives into raw data. Applied to L1s and L2s, the metrics show a modest decline in the top three networks' (Ethereum, Solana, and Base) combined market share and highlight volatility driven by bridge flows and token performance.

title: Solo Founder Syndrome

title: PeerDAS means Real Sharding Finally Live on Ethereum
text: PeerDAS means Real Sharding Finally Live on Ethereum. Fusaka's PeerDAS is sharding. Ethereum now reaches consensus on blocks without any single node having to see all the data, using client-side sampling instead of validator voting. That means L2s can scale to far more data and transactions while keeping Ethereum's security guarantees, and this is even robust to 51% attacks. The L1, however, still doesn't benefit fully without mature zkEVMs, block building is still centralized in builders, and there's no sharded mempool yet. Over the next two years, the plan is to scale up PeerDAS for L2s, then point it inward to increase L1 gas capacity once zkEVMs are ready.

title: Understanding RCPs and SCPs in AWS: Choosing the Right Policy for your Security Needs
text: Understanding RCPs and SCPs in AWS: Choosing the Right Policy for your Security Needs. Resource Control Policies (RCPs) were introduced by AWS in late 2024 to restrict access to resources within your AWS organization. RCPs should be used when trying to enforce consistent security standards in supported services, you're running out of SCPs, or you want to use the NotResource condition. SCPs should be used when you want to apply a policy to services that are not supported by RCPs or use the NotAction condition.

title: What Framing Security Alerts as a Binary True or False Positive is Costing You
text: What Framing Security Alerts as a Binary True or False Positive is Costing You. The true/false positive binaries only capture whether an alert accurately alerts on a signal, but not if it is impactful. The author proposes adding a layer of intent or disposition to alert metrics to measure whether the alert had an actual business impact. This leads to a refined classification with the steps: True Positive - Malicious, True Positive - Suspicious, True Positive - No Business Impact, and False Positive.

title: A Browser Extension Risk Guide After the ShadyPanda Campaign
text: A Browser Extension Risk Guide After the ShadyPanda Campaign. The ShadyPanda campaign compromised popular Chrome and Edge extensions after seven years of building trust, affecting 4.3 million users, and installed full RCE frameworks that could steal session tokens and hijack SaaS accounts, bypassing MFA. The attack demonstrated that browser extensions represent a critical blind spot between endpoint and cloud security, with silent auto-updates enabling overnight weaponization. Organizations should implement extension allow lists, treat extension permissions as OAuth access grants, conduct regular permission audits, and monitor for behavioral changes that indicate compromise.

title: Senate Begins Passage of Stablecoin Bill
text: Senate Begins Passage of Stablecoin Bill. The Senate cleared a 68-30 cloture vote on the GENIUS Act, virtually guaranteeing final passage of the first US stablecoin bill and ending years of Senate gridlock on crypto policy. The legislation, which aims to strengthen dollar dominance, will let state- or federally-supervised entities issue dollar-backed tokens under a unified framework. Parallel momentum in the House saw the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act pass through both Financial Services and Agriculture Committees, setting up potential coordination or reconciliation between the bills. Together, the advances mark the strongest bipartisan push yet toward comprehensive U.S. crypto regulation.

title: When Updates Backfire: RCE in Windows Update Health Tools
text: When Updates Backfire: RCE in Windows Update Health Tools. text: Lido Dual Governance Live on Mainnet. Lido DAO launched a dual governance system that grants stETH holders veto rights over protocol decisions. Holders representing at least 1% of total staked ETH can delay proposals for five days by staking stETH into an escrow contract. If dissent reaches 10% of staked ETH, the DAO enters “rage-quit” mode, freezing new proposals until conflicts are resolved. This design introduces an onchain check-and-balance to safeguard liquid stakers against potentially adverse governance actions.

title: AWS Certificate Manager Has Announced Exportable TLS Certificates, and I'm Mostly Okay With It
text: AWS Certificate Manager Has Announced Exportable TLS Certificates, and I'm Mostly Okay With It. Recently, AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) added the ability to export certificates and private keys for use in EC2 or external applications. Currently, ACM does not implement ACME, so certificate renewals must be handled manually or via the AWS API. Organizations that want to ensure that private keys never leave ACM can disable exports at the AWS Organization level or on an account-by-account basis via SCPs.

title: Quitting Trump's top crypto job wasn't easy
text: Quitting Trump's top crypto job wasn't easy. Bo Hines stepped down as head of Trump's digital assets council on August 9 after delivering a comprehensive 180-day report positioning the US as the "crypto capital of the world," feeling confident the groundwork and regulatory framework were established while clearing the way for deputy Patrick Witt to lead upcoming legislative battles, including the digital asset market structure bill. Within days of his resignation, Hines received over 50 job offers before being appointed CEO of Tether USA on August 19, where he's overseeing the launch of USAT, which he claims will be the first federally licensed stablecoin in the US. The 30-year-old brings nearly a decade of crypto engagement since age 18 to his current role at Tether, which accounts for over half of all daily crypto transaction settlements. Hines remains confident in the Strategic Bitcoin Reserve's progress despite no timeline, praising crypto czar David Sacks' leadership, while envisioning a future of 24/7 markets, instant settlement, and tokenized public securities where "everything moves onchain," with stablecoin market cap potentially exceeding Treasury Secretary Bessent's trillion-dollar projection as tokenization accelerates globally.

title: Blob Fees, L2s, and Why Blobs Are Just Getting Started
text: Blob Fees, L2s, and Why Blobs Are Just Getting Started. If all Ethereum blobs were sold at the average per-byte rate paid by perp appchain Lighter over the past three days, L1 blob revenue would be about 5x higher (~$52 million per year), highlighting early but growing, relatively price-insensitive demand for data availability. Lighter currently pays 6x more per blob than Base (13x more per byte once normalized for fullness) while spending a smaller share of its L2 revenue on blobs thanks to highly optimized Data availability (DA) and zk state diffs, suggesting that efficient, high-margin L2s could drive blob fees up and force less efficient rollups toward higher costs or alternative DA, with blob revenue becoming “icing on the cake” for ETH.

title: Tests should not contain logic
text: Tests should not contain logic. Tests should avoid logic to prevent masking bugs in the code under test—duplicating the same flawed logic in a test will lead to false positives. Robinhood became the first public US company to offer tokenized stocks via Arbitrum, while Deutsche Bank unveiled plans for a zkSync-based tokenization platform, and Coinbase and Kraken filed with regulators for onchain equities offerings. ETH gained nearly 6% on the week as companies like BitMine and Bit Digital shifted treasury reserves into ETH, and ether ETFs recorded back-to-back inflows, underpinned by stablecoin settlement dominance on Ethereum. title: JavaScript scope hoisting is broken
text: JavaScript scope hoisting is broken. JavaScript scope hoisting is an optimization used by bundlers to concatenate modules into a single scope instead of wrapping each in a function, but it breaks when combined with code splitting because it can't preserve the correct execution order of modules with side effects. This leads to different program behavior, with shared modules executing in the wrong order compared to unbundled code, affecting bundlers like Rollup, ESBuild, and others. The solution requires wrapping modules in functions to control execution order.

title: Stablecoins Do Create Credit, Just Not Like Banks
text: Stablecoins Do Create Credit, Just Not Like Banks. Stablecoins, such as Circle's USDC, actively create credit through their reserves, including treasuries (loans to the government), repos (secured loans to financial institutions), and bank deposits (loans to banks). This structure qualifies as credit creation, even without fractional lending. Banks account for just 20% of U.S. credit, with trillions more generated through money market funds and securitized debt like MBS. Narrow-bank stablecoins are less prone to runs, need no bailouts, and offer safer intermediation, making them a financial upgrade, not a threat.

title: Memory Integrity Enforcement: A complete vision for memory safety in Apple devices
text: Memory Integrity Enforcement: A complete vision for memory safety in Apple devices. Apple's Memory Integrity Enforcement (MIE), introduced in the iPhone 17 and iPhone Air, combines secure memory allocators with Enhanced Memory Tagging Extension (EMTE) hardware to provide continuous memory safety against advanced spyware exploiting memory corruption. It uses hardware-enforced memory tagging to prevent buffer overflows and use-after-free attacks by assigning secret tags to memory and terminating processes when tags are incorrect. Tag Confidentiality Enforcement also defends against side-channel attacks, including Spectre V1 mitigations. Apple's tests show MIE defeats all recent real-world exploits, forcing attackers to restart development with new techniques and making memory corruption exploits more difficult and costly.

title: ARR Doesn't Need a Redefinition
text: ARR Doesn't Need a Redefinition. Everyone's panicking about how to measure ARR with AI's usage-based pricing, but ARR was never that clean. Snowflake is entirely usage-based yet valued as recurring revenue. Datadog mixes flat fees with variable metrics. Even traditional SaaS has messy pricing - customers can be charged by seats, active users, and usage tiers all at once. What makes revenue "recurring" isn't whether it's fixed monthly, it's whether you can predict it with confidence. Your job is to define ARR honestly for your business model, not game it to fit some perfect definition.

title: No, AI Progress is Not Grinding to a Halt
text: No, AI Progress is Not Grinding to a Halt. Everyone's declaring GPT-5 a disappointment that proves AI progress is hitting a wall, but they're measuring it wrong. When GPT-4 launched in 2023, it was compared to technology from 2020 - of course, it looked revolutionary. Now GPT-5 is being compared to models released last month. If GPT-4 came out today, it would be dead on arrival. It handled 8,200 tokens versus GPT-5's 400,000, cost 10x more to run, and scored 25/100 on benchmarks where GPT-5 gets 69. On real-world coding tests, GPT-4 solved 2.8% of problems while GPT-5 solved 65%. GPT-4 can handle 5-minute human tasks - GPT-5 can handle tasks that are more than two hours. However, because everyone expected another ChatGPT moment and OpenAI bungled the release, the narrative has solidified in DC that AI is stalling.

title: Medusa Ransomware Claims Comcast Data Breach, Demands $1.2M
text: Medusa Ransomware Claims Comcast Data Breach, Demands $1.2M. The Medusa ransomware group claims to have exfiltrated 834.4 GB of data from Comcast Corporation and is demanding $1.2 million either as ransom for deletion or as a sale price to interested buyers. As proof, the attackers posted approximately 20 screenshots of alleged internal files, along with a listing of 167,121 file entries containing actuarial reports, insurance modeling scripts, claim analytics, and product management data, including files such as Python and SQL scripts related to auto premium analysis. Comcast has not confirmed or denied the breach. It experienced a credential leak in 2015 that affected over 200,000 users. The breach was attributed to credential aggregation and not a system breach.

title: Amazon's Exit from Google Ads Triggered a Traffic Surge — But ROI Didn't Follow
text: Amazon's Exit from Google Ads Triggered a Traffic Surge — But ROI Didn't Follow. Amazon's sudden exit from Google Shopping ads in July caused immediate disruption across ad auctions, leading to more clicks and impressions but weaker returns for most advertisers. Overall clicks rose 7.8% and CPCs fell 8.3%, but conversion value declined 5.5% and ROAS dropped 4.4%, showing that extra traffic often failed to translate into profitable sales. Electronics was the only major category to see true gains, with conversions up 81.3% and ROAS up 7.1%, as brands like Best Buy and Apple could match Amazon's speed and pricing. Most other sectors, including Apparel & Accessories and Sporting Goods, fell into the “volume trap,” gaining traffic but losing efficiency as Amazon-seeking shoppers expected unmatched price and convenience. Advertisers must track value, not just volume, and focus on sustainable advantages rather than chasing displaced Amazon traffic.

title: asyncmcp
