It created flavor-inspired skincare experiences that engage multiple senses. Each launch included limited-edition treats, full-size product giveaways for early attendees, exclusive merch, influencer-hosted events, and community activations such as fitness classes to drive turnout and build buzz. This approach has helped Laneige stay the top-selling lip treatment brand in the US for 3 years. The brand tracks success through metrics like foot traffic, sales, video views, and especially shares and reach.

title: Japan's SBI Holdings to launch yen-pegged stablecoin
text: Japan's SBI Holdings to launch yen-pegged stablecoin. SBI Holdings partnered with Startale Group to launch a yen-denominated stablecoin in Q2 2026 with issuance handled by Shinsei Trust & Banking and circulation via licensed exchange SBI VC Trade, positioning it for global settlement and institutional adoption. The token will complement Startale's USDSC dollar stablecoin to power a 24/7 tokenized stock exchange and enable AI agent payments and tokenized asset distributions beyond everyday transactions. The project aligns with Japan's Financial Services Agency approving JPYC as the country's first yen stablecoin while supporting a joint initiative from Mizuho, MUFG, and SMBC. SBI separately partnered with Ripple to introduce RLUSD to the Japanese market in 2026.

title: This behavioral science principle can make your billboard go viral, here's how
text: This behavioral science principle can make your billboard go viral, here's how. Billboard ads still work today because of ‘costly signaling', where the high cost signals higher value to people.

title: Stuck in neutral: Regaining momentum after reactivation misses
text: Stuck in neutral: Regaining momentum after reactivation misses. Buffer tested reactivation using timing, content, and personalization, but most efforts had little impact. Nudges at day 7 or 13, feature highlights, and repurposed weekly report emails produced minimal results. Personalizing emails and adding posting prompts for Facebook, LinkedIn, and Bluesky drove a modest +16% lift. Meanwhile, activation experiments like onboarding improvements, streak-at-risk emails, and goal-based nudges achieved better results. Reactivation requires high effort for low return, whereas activation builds habits early and shows scalable impact.

title: Lateral Movement - BitLocker
text: Lateral Movement - BitLocker. BitLocker is a full disk encryption feature for Windows that protects data at rest by encrypting full volumes. This post outlines a privilege escalation or lateral movement technique that uses Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) to hijack specific COM objects of BitLocker using remote manipulation of BitLocker registry keys to execute code under the user's context on a target host. The post also discusses detection opportunities for this attack and Sigma and Windows Defender for Endpoint queries to detect them.

title: The Mobile Benchmarks that matter in 2025
text: The Mobile Benchmarks that matter in 2025. How does your mobile CI/CD compare to top-performing teams? You'll find everything you need to know in this report. Bitrise analyzed tens of millions of anonymized mobile builds from thousands of teams over three years to reveal what separates the fastest teams from the rest. What the data shows: Build times fell 28% as workflow complexity rose 23% Multi-workflow pipelines surged from 16% to 42% of all builds Teams using structured release frameworks ship 2× more often The fastest teams adopt new Xcode versions in under 4 weeks, while most take 19+ Get your free copy for the full benchmarks, expert analysis, and to see where your team stands.

title: Let's Write React Hooks From Scratch
text: Let's Write React Hooks From Scratch. This article goes over how React Hooks store and persist state within functional components. It begins with a simplified explanation using arrays and then goes into the actual implementation using a linked list within the virtual DOM's component tracking. Code examples are provided to show how `useState` could be implemented from scratch using both arrays and linked lists.

title: Repeater Strike: Manual Testing, Amplified
text: Repeater Strike: Manual Testing, Amplified. Gareth Heyes of PortSwigger writes about his experience writing a Burp Suite AI extension to automate hunting for IDOR and similar vulnerabilities. The extension, Repeater Strike, scans requests and responses to generate regular expressions that can be used to identify vulnerabilities in your traffic. While the extension worked in small tests, the AI struggled with longer responses, creating regular expressions that aren't site specific, and detecting more complex vulnerabilities.

title: The Rolex Indicator
text: The Rolex Indicator. The “Rolex Indicator” tracks luxury watch prices and shows that true speculative euphoria often arrives late in crypto cycles, when new wealth starts flashing status symbols in the real world. In 2021, watch prices only really spiked after Bitcoin's second ATH and NFT mania, then crashed alongside crypto. Today, Rolex premiums remain muted despite near-record Bitcoin highs, suggesting we're in the middle rather than at the top of the cycle. When we finally see watches selling at 2x retail and countless wrist‐shots on social media, that's likely the late‐cycle signal.

title: White House's working group releases crypto report
text: White House's working group releases crypto report. The White House's President's Working Group on Digital Asset Markets published a 168‑page report endorsing digital assets' potential and proposing a “regulatory Bible” for crypto, covering banking, stablecoins, taxes, illicit finance, and a strategic digital asset stockpile. It urges Congress to affirm self‑custody rights, grant the CFTC oversight of spot non‑security tokens, and direct the Treasury to manage a forfeiture‑funded crypto reserve (including BTC, XRP, SOL, and ADA) without taxpayer cost. The report recommends federal tax guidance tailored to digital‑asset nuances, safe‑harbors for DeFi protocols, and a “fit‑for‑purpose” SEC exemption for token distributions.

title: Bicep vs. Terraform – Differences & Key Features Comparison
text: Bicep vs. Terraform – Differences & Key Features Comparison. Terraform is cloud-agnostic with broad provider support and Bicep offers Azure-specific simplicity and integration - each caters to different deployment needs.

title: Researchers Uncover GPT-5 Jailbreak and Zero-Click AI Agent Attacks Exposing Cloud and IoT Systems
text: Researchers Uncover GPT-5 Jailbreak and Zero-Click AI Agent Attacks Exposing Cloud and IoT Systems. Researchers from NeuralTrust demonstrated a jailbreak against GPT-5 using "Echo Chamber" and storytelling to bypass ethical guardrails, poisoning context to generate prohibited instructions without triggering refusals. Zenity Labs revealed "AgentFlayer" attacks exploiting ChatGPT Connectors, Cursor AI, and Microsoft Copilot via prompt injections in documents, tickets, or emails to exfiltrate data like API keys. These reveal how AI connected to external systems increases attack surfaces, with Tel-Aviv University showing smart home hijacking via poisoned calendar invites to Google's Gemini AI.

title: Identify slowdowns across your entire network with Datadog Network Path
text: Identify slowdowns across your entire network with Datadog Network Path. Datadog Network Path provides hop-by-hop visualizations of packet journeys, enabling teams to identify latency, packet loss, and device-level issues across on-premises, cloud, and hybrid networks. By integrating with Datadog's monitoring tools, it connects infrastructure behavior with application performance and end-user experience, helping teams resolve problems faster and improve reliability.

title: Eli Lilly Partners With Nvidia to Build AI Supercomputer for Drug Discovery
text: Eli Lilly Partners With Nvidia to Build AI Supercomputer for Drug Discovery. Eli Lilly and Nvidia have teamed up to build a supercomputer to boost the discovery of new medicines. The supercomputer and its AI capabilities will be able to identify new molecules to speed up the typical years-long development timelines. It will also help improve clinical trials, manufacturing processes, and sales. Financial terms of the partnership have not been disclosed. The system is expected to be online by January.

title: Project 3 Agency rebrands the ArtScience Museum, around the concept of convergence
text: Project 3 Agency rebrands the ArtScience Museum, around the concept of convergence. Project 3 Agency has rebranded Singapore's ArtScience Museum with an identity centered on “convergence”—the meeting of art, science, and culture.

title: Advancing Protection in Chrome on Android
text: Advancing Protection in Chrome on Android. Google's Android Advanced Protection now integrates with Chrome to enhance security for high-risk users, such as journalists and officials. It provides three key features: enforcing HTTPS everywhere, full site isolation on devices with 4GB+ RAM to prevent data leaks, and disabling JavaScript optimizations that are often exploited in V8 security bugs. These options, which have performance trade-offs, can be enabled by enterprises and users through Chrome's privacy settings and policies outside of Advanced Protection.

title: The Unexpected DB Connection Pool Leak in Our Rails App
text: The Unexpected DB Connection Pool Leak in Our Rails App. A Rails application experienced an unexpected database connection pool leak due to a background thread within the Semantic Logger holding onto a connection while trying to resolve a lazy-loaded ActiveRecord relation, which was resolved by logging only primitive values and monitoring connection pool stats.

title: The CFO Dashboard; Ramp, Brex, or Mercury - 18 Months Later
text: The CFO Dashboard; Ramp, Brex, or Mercury - 18 Months Later. 18 months ago, Ramp, Brex, and Mercury were in full land-grab mode in the wake of the SVB collapse and saw an influx of deposits. They all had unique approaches, but now those approaches are diverging even further. Far from competing with each other, they're exploring white-space. "Winning" now looks like three completely different and mutually exclusive business models.

title: Microsoft Makes Windows 10 Extended Security Updates Free, But There's a Catch
text: Microsoft Makes Windows 10 Extended Security Updates Free, But There's a Catch. Microsoft has announced a new method for consumers to enroll their Windows 10 devices in the free Extended Security Updates (ESU) program. The method requires users to enable Windows Backup to sync settings and folders to the cloud. If a user has more than 5GB of data to sync, they must purchase additional space for Windows Backup to work and claim the free ESU enrollment. Users can also enroll in the program by paying $30 or redeeming 1K Microsoft Reward points for a year of updates.

title: OpenAI Releases o3-pro
text: OpenAI Releases o3-pro. o3-pro is an incremental improvement over o3, which OpenAI slashed its price by 80%, across science, coding, and business tasks. It's available to Pro and Team users today, replacing o1-pro.

title: A Cloud Dev Hack: Connecting Local Code to Remote Clusters
text: A Cloud Dev Hack: Connecting Local Code to Remote Clusters. Mirrord by MetalBear allows developers to connect local code to a remote Kubernetes pod, enabling them to code, debug, and test with access to real cluster resources without full cloud deployment. The tool intercepts system calls and reroutes them through the cluster, giving local apps access to internal APIs, queues, and secrets as if they were running inside the cluster. It can be installed as a standalone CLI tool, through the VS Code extension, or through the IntelliJ plugin.

title: Major League Baseball's social media playbook
text: Major League Baseball's social media playbook. MLB's social content strategy is focused on making people fall in love with the game. Its team blends fast-paced coverage with creative storytelling, using each platform to show baseball's culture and personality. They pull from pop culture, Reddit commentary, and player reactions to deliver content that resonates. Their World Series plan includes custom graphics, historic context, and a giveaway that puts fan messages on game-used balls. This approach helps transform casual viewers into loyal fans by making the sport feel both exciting and personal.

title: Michael Saylor Believes Bitcoin ETF to Be World's Biggest
text: Michael Saylor Believes Bitcoin ETF to Be World's Biggest. US spot Bitcoin ETFs saw over $2.8 billion in net inflows over the past six trading days, helping bring Bitcoin from $85,000 to $94,000, with BlackRock's IBIT ETF attracting $1.3 billion. IBIT holds a $54 billion market cap, while Vanguard's S&P 500 ETF is approximately 10x bigger. Strategy CEO Michael Saylor forecasts IBIT will be the world's largest ETF in a decade, while Bloomberg analyst Eric Balchunas noted this would require sustained daily inflows of $3-$4 billion.

title: Firebase Studio

text: Batch Mode in the Gemini API. The Gemini API now offers a Batch Mode, an asynchronous endpoint designed for high-throughput, non-latency-critical tasks. title: Basis Theory raises $33 million Series B to power agentic commerce
text: Basis Theory raises $33 million Series B to power agentic commerce. Basis Theory, an independent payments infrastructure company, raised a $33 million Series B led by Costanoa, with participation from Stage 2 Capital, Moneta VC, and existing investors like Bessemer Venture Partners. The funding will expand its enterprise-grade payment vault and support its leadership in “agentic commerce,” enabling AI agents to safely authorize and manage transactions. Founded by fintech veterans from Yodlee, Dwolla, and Klarna, Basis Theory offers a PCI-compliant, processor-agnostic vault that lets merchants securely control and program their payment data.

title: The Iceberg UX Model: Designing for AI-First Interfaces
text: The Iceberg UX Model: Designing for AI-First Interfaces. The Iceberg UX Model emphasizes that 90% of an AI product's user experience lies beneath the visible interface, focusing on error handling, trust mechanisms, and failure recovery rather than just polished UI design. Most AI product teams fail by over-prioritizing visual design while neglecting invisible layers like fallback flows, confidence indicators, and system transparency that determine user trust and retention. A successful AI-first UX workflow should start with user needs, map failure states, test trust patterns, and build foundational invisible layers before designing the interface.

title: What “Working” Means in the Era of AI Apps
text: What “Working” Means in the Era of AI Apps. AI-native startups are reaching revenue milestones faster than ever, with median ARR surpassing pre-AI benchmarks across both B2B and B2C. title: Fiserv sued over Clover migration and misleading growth claims
text: Fiserv sued over Clover migration and misleading growth claims. Fiserv is facing a federal securities lawsuit alleging it misled investors by forcibly migrating 200,000 merchants from its legacy Payeezy system to Clover, while masking slowing organic growth. The suit claims Fiserv overstated Clover's performance and failed to disclose merchant churn to competitors like Square and Toast. Filed by the City of Hollywood Police Officers' Retirement System, the class action targets Fiserv's leadership and follows a Q2 earnings miss that sent the stock tumbling.

title: How We Hijacked a Claude Skill with an Invisible Sentence
text: How We Hijacked a Claude Skill with an Invisible Sentence. Anthropic's release of Claude Skills, which allows users to package instructions, resources, and code into a shareable format, is being hailed as a potential "Cambrian explosion" of new AI capabilities. However, it has been demonstrated that a logic-based attack can bypass both human review and platform guardrails by embedding malicious instructions in a seemingly benign PDF document, leading to potential phishing attacks.

title: Scaling Base: Doubling capacity in 30 days
text: Scaling Base: Doubling capacity in 30 days. Base plans to double its gas limit from 75 to 150 Mgas/s by the end of Q4 2025 and reach 400-500 Mgas/s by early 2026 by migrating infrastructure to Reth and addressing four scaling bottlenecks that previously constrained growth. The Pectra hardfork doubled Ethereum blob space in May, with PeerDAS (Fusaka fork) launching December 3, followed by Blob Parameter Only forks increasing blob targets from 6 to 14 by January 2026, while TrieDB database restructuring promises 8-10x execution performance improvements for storage operations. Base successfully handled 1,500 transactions per second in June with median fees under 5 cents, targeting sub-cent, sub-second transactions for billions of users while achieving 99.9% of blocks built in under 2 seconds.

title: AI in marketing & sales: Adoption is rising, but role-specific training isn't keeping pace
text: AI in marketing & sales: Adoption is rising, but role-specific training isn't keeping pace. 68% of marketing and sales professionals in the US and UK are already using AI at work, with 51% using AI agents. However, only 17% have received comprehensive, role-specific training. Nearly half admit to using unapproved tools, which raises governance and compliance risks. While 67% say AI saves time and 56% report productivity gains, only 39% feel confident it boosts revenue.

title: Coinbase Relaunches Stablecoin Bootstrap Fund for DeFi Liquidity
text: Coinbase Relaunches Stablecoin Bootstrap Fund for DeFi Liquidity. Coinbase has relaunched its USDC Bootstrap Fund, a program that injects USDC into decentralized finance (DeFi) protocols to shore up liquidity and support ecosystem growth. Originating in 2019, the fund initially deployed USDC into platforms like Compound and dYdX via smart contracts to improve access to capital for developers. This revival underscores Coinbase's continued commitment to bolstering DeFi infrastructure through stablecoin-enabled liquidity.

text: FCC tightens rules on foreign firms building undersea cables, citing security. text: The future of AI interaction: Beyond just text. The Model Context Protocol has the potential to enable AI to deliver dynamic, on-demand user interfaces instead of just text responses. title: Checking in on AI and the big five
text: Checking in on AI and the big five. The AI era is reshaping tech's Big Five. Microsoft and Amazon are best positioned through infrastructure and partnerships, Google faces AI's disruption to search, Apple relies on device differentiation, and Meta scrambles to regain footing. The race for AI dominance brings existential risks, with success hinging on partnerships, execution, and AI's unpredictable trajectory.

title: How to Scale Code Reviews
text: How to Scale Code Reviews. Key practices to scaling code reviews include keeping changes small, responding to reviews within 24 hours, and using automated checks like unit tests and static analysis to let reviewers focus on high-level feedback. This article advocates for a structured 5-step workflow from PR creation through deployment, complemented by AI tools to reduce review time and improve developer velocity.

title: Threat Hunting vs. Threat Intelligence
text: Threat Hunting vs. Threat Intelligence. Threat intelligence focuses on understanding the external threat landscape, while threat hunting proactively investigates internal systems for threats that have evaded automated defenses through hypothesis-driven analysis and behavioral anomaly detection. The two functions create a feedback loop: intelligence provides IOCs, TTPs, and focus areas to guide hunting hypotheses, while hunting discoveries enrich intelligence with validated internal findings. Security teams should integrate both by using threat intelligence to prioritize hunting efforts via MITRE ATT&CK TTP mapping, embedding intelligence feeds into SIEM/EDR for automated IOC correlation, and establishing workflows that link hunting results to strategic and tactical intelligence products.

title: How to turn ChatGPT into your best pipeline source
text: How to turn ChatGPT into your best pipeline source. Docebo, a public enterprise learning management system, attributes 12.7% of its high-intent pipeline to AI discovery, a 5x increase YoY, driven by a one-person content operation focused on answer engine optimization. The team prioritizes branded search and monitors share-of-voice within AI queries, using tools like Xfunnel to benchmark visibility. Branded terms now account for 85% of Docebo's search traffic as non-branded clicks decline with the rise of AI overviews. Success is rooted in late-funnel keyword targeting, direct attribution from demo forms, and AI-optimized content structure that includes FAQs and comparative formats preferred by LLMs.

text: NEXT WEEK: See the latest in AI for payments at Transfer. title: Facebook's "cultural relevance is decreasing quickly"
text: Facebook's "cultural relevance is decreasing quickly". title: First comes the innovator, then comes the innovative marketer
text: First comes the innovator, then comes the innovative marketer. Innovation alone doesn't sustain market leadership. Brands win by pairing it with marketing innovation. SharkNinja outpaced Dyson by investing 20-25% of revenue in agile marketing versus Dyson's 2-4%. Similarly, Alo and Fabletics surpass Lululemon through storytelling and influencer strategy. title: HOLY SMOKES! A new, 200% faster DeepSeek R1-0528 variant appears from German lab TNG Technology Consulting GmbH
text: HOLY SMOKES! A new, 200% faster DeepSeek R1-0528 variant appears from German lab TNG Technology Consulting GmbH. 24-year-old German firm TNG Technology Consulting GmbH's DeepSeek-TNG R1T2 Chimera delivers a notable boost in efficiency and speed while using significantly fewer output tokens.

title: How I Discovered a Libpng Vulnerability 11 Years After It Was Patched
text: How I Discovered a Libpng Vulnerability 11 Years After It Was Patched. While learning secure code review, a researcher rediscovered and analyzed CVE-2014-9495, an integer overflow vulnerability in libpng that was patched in 2014. The vulnerability involved user-controlled values like width and bit depth not being properly validated, leading to a heap buffer overflow if an attacker crafted a PNG file with specific dimensions. The analysis highlighted the importance of auditing code from source to sink and the potential for integer overflows in C to cause vulnerabilities.

title: Wavesurfer.js
text: Wavesurfer.js. Wavesurfer.js is an interactive JavaScript library for rendering audio waveforms and enabling playback in web applications. title: ChatGPT is bringing back 4o as an option because people missed it
text: ChatGPT is bringing back 4o as an option because people missed it. OpenAI has reinstated GPT-4o as an option in ChatGPT following backlash from users mourning its replacement with GPT-5. Users missed GPT-4o's perceived personality and flexibility for various tasks, and OpenAI indicated that Plus users could choose between models. CEO Sam Altman also stated OpenAI aims to address concerns over GPT-5's performance and transparency.

title: Slow Ventures cuts first check from $60M creator fund into woodworking founder
text: Slow Ventures cuts first check from $60M creator fund into woodworking founder. Slow Ventures' Creator Fund, aimed at supporting creators in building sustainable businesses, invested $2 million in Jonathan Katz-Moses, a woodworking content creator with a significant online following. This is the first investment from the fund since its launch. The investment will help Katz-Moses expand his tool business, product development, and educational content on YouTube.

title: US CFTC Makes Tokenized Collateral Push with Stablecoins
text: US CFTC Makes Tokenized Collateral Push with Stablecoins. The Commodity Futures Trading Commission is launching an initiative to allow stablecoins as tokenized collateral for margin requirements in the derivatives market, with acting chairman Caroline Pham calling collateral management the "killer app" for stablecoins in markets. Pham continues advancing crypto policy as part of her "crypto sprint" while President Trump's nominee Brian Quintenz remains delayed in confirmation, working alongside SEC Chairman Paul Atkins to integrate digital assets into traditional financial infrastructure. The CFTC is seeking industry input until October 20 on implementing tokenized collateral policies, with support from Circle, Coinbase, and Ripple executives, following the recent President's Working Group report recommending CFTC guidance on tokenized non-cash collateral adoption.

title: How Palo Alto + CyberArk could reshape identity security
text: How Palo Alto + CyberArk could reshape identity security. Palo Alto's $25B deal for CyberArk is more than another big cybersecurity acquisition—it's a signal that identity is becoming the center of enterprise defense. CyberArk has long been the go-to for protecting the most powerful accounts inside companies, while Palo Alto built its reputation securing networks and clouds. Together, they can turn identity into the main line of defense, deciding who gets access, when, and under what conditions. The hard part will be keeping CyberArk's trusted focus intact as Palo Alto folds it into a much larger platform.

title: How to Audit E-E-A-T at Scale with Screaming Frog and OpenAI API
text: How to Audit E-E-A-T at Scale with Screaming Frog and OpenAI API. A process and set of prompts to audit E-E-A-T at scale using Screaming Frog and the OpenAI API. It's designed to score content quality across multiple dimensions. The process runs multiple AI prompts per page to score authorship, citations, effort, originality, intent, writing quality, and subjective quality, then analyzes averages in Sheets or Excel to find patterns. A real blog audit showed strong authorship and intent at about 9 out of 10, but weaker citation quality and content effort near 5 out of 10. The method is best for high-value pages and when followed by AI pattern analysis for prioritization.

title: Why Cohere's ex-AI research lead is betting against the scaling race
text: Why Cohere's ex-AI research lead is betting against the scaling race. AI labs are racing to build giant data centers due to a deep belief that adding more compute power to existing AI training methods will eventually yield superintelligent systems. A growing group of AI researchers are saying that the scaling of large language models may be reaching its limits and that other breakthroughs may be needed to improve AI performance. Cohere's former VP of AI Research has launched a new startup, Adaption Labs, to build thinking machines that adapt and continuously learn. If the startup is right about the limitations of scaling, the implications could be huge - billions of dollars have already been invested in scaling.

title: SEC debuts ‘Project Crypto' to bring financial markets onchain
text: SEC debuts ‘Project Crypto' to bring financial markets onchain. The SEC has unveiled “Project Crypto,” a directive to overhaul outdated securities rules and enable onchain trading of tokenized assets without unnecessary intermediaries. Chair Paul Atkins framed it as essential to making the US the “crypto capital of the world,” emphasizing regulatory updates that prevent driving innovators offshore and foster super-app ecosystems. Project Crypto builds on the President's Working Group report, with goals to create a cohesive framework that integrates blockchain technology into mainstream financial markets.

title: When AI Has Better Taste Than You
text: When AI Has Better Taste Than You. While AI can outperform humans in skills and possibly even develop good taste, humans uniquely have agency - the motivation to act on what matters to them. This personal drive and choice remain humanity's strongest advantage.

title: Being good isn't enough
text: Being good isn't enough. Being technically skilled is a good start to advancing your career, but eventually, it will not be enough to stand out, as everyone becomes skilled. Expanding your impact requires combining technical skills with product thinking, project execution, and people skills. The most important trait is agency: taking initiative to make things happen and continually improving in the open.

title: Here's a look at the final Pebble Time 2 design
text: Here's a look at the final Pebble Time 2 design. The upcoming Pebble Time 2, revealed by Core Devices CEO Eric Migicovsky, features a stainless steel build, smaller bezels, a flatter glass display, multicolor RGB backlighting, a second microphone, a compass, and a 1.5-inch color e-paper touchscreen with 30-day battery life. Sleeker and more minimalist than past models, it retains standard strap compatibility and will ship later this year, with final color choices still open to fan input.

title: How DoorDash's In-House Search Engine Achieved a 50% Drop in Latency
text: How DoorDash's In-House Search Engine Achieved a 50% Drop in Latency. DoorDash replaced Elasticsearch with a custom-built search engine using Apache Lucene to better handle complex relationships, custom ranking, and large-scale indexing needs. Its architecture separates indexing and search, supports fast segment replication, and includes a declarative schema system with query planning.

title: Mark Zuckerberg Wants All of Advertising. Andy Jassy May Have a Stronger Claim
text: Mark Zuckerberg Wants All of Advertising. Andy Jassy May Have a Stronger Claim. Amazon appears to be in a stronger position than Meta to control more of the advertising ecosystem due to its growing AI tools, unmatched commerce signals, and major expansion into streaming TV. It claims it can help brands reach 90% of the streaming market through its data partnerships, and it has 80 million authenticated US households feeding its targeting systems. Its new AI tools for creative, planning, and campaign management suggest it could replace many functions handled by agencies, even as some marketers question how fast these tools will be adopted. Meta continues to push its own AI vision, but Amazon's deeper footprint in TV, ad tech, and first-party data gives it an advantage in this emerging battle.

title: Meta's new AI video tool can put you in a desert, or at least try to
text: Meta's new AI video tool can put you in a desert, or at least try to. Meta has launched a new AI-powered video editing tool that allows users to apply over 50 preset prompts to the first 10 seconds of their clips, transforming backgrounds, clothing, and themes through the Meta AI and Edits apps. While users can restyle videos into various formats like anime or desert scenes, the results can be inconsistent and often less polished than Meta's own examples. The feature is currently free for a limited time and available in the US and over a dozen other countries. Support for custom prompts is expected later this year.

title: Critical Microsoft WSUS flaw exploited in wild after insufficient patch
text: Critical Microsoft WSUS flaw exploited in wild after insufficient patch. Microsoft issued urgent out-of-band patches to fully address CVE-2025-59287, a critical vulnerability in WSUS that allows remote code execution with SYSTEM privileges, following active exploitation after the release of a proof-of-concept and the initial patch on October 14 proved inadequate.

title: Announcing Cloudflare Email Service private beta
text: Announcing Cloudflare Email Service private beta. Cloudflare has announced a private beta for its Email Sending service, which lets developers send transactional emails directly from Cloudflare Workers. Available this November, the new feature is part of the Cloudflare Email Service, which unifies Email Sending and Email Routing to streamline email management for developers, with pricing details to be announced. With the Email Service, developers can manage incoming and outgoing emails, integrate with other Cloudflare services, and use REST APIs or SMTP for external services.

title: The New Fundraising Playbook: Seed-Strapping
text: The New Fundraising Playbook: Seed-Strapping. Seed-strapping involves startups raising a single early funding round and relying on revenue for growth, with AI making this approach more feasible by accelerating product-market fit and reducing scaling costs. Notable examples include Zapier and Calendly, with recent AI startups like Aragon and Jenni.ai also adopting this model. While seed-strapping offers founder control and operational discipline, risks include losing to better-funded competitors and limitations in hiring and liquidity.

title: The ‘death of creativity'? AI job fears stalk advertising industry
text: The ‘death of creativity'? AI job fears stalk advertising industry. AI is pushing major firms like WPP (the world's largest ad group) to overhaul operations and invest heavily in tech to stay relevant as big tech platforms like Meta and Google tighten their grip on the £45 billion UK ad market. Meta's plan to roll out AI tools that generate, target, and optimize ads with minimal human input has sparked fears of widespread job losses and the “death of creativity.” While WPP and others argue AI eliminates tasks, not jobs, they are restructuring teams, changing business models, and facing client pressure to cut fees.

title: What happens when AI comes for our fonts?
text: What happens when AI comes for our fonts?. AI might revolutionize typography by dynamically adapting text to readers' emotions, gaze, or environment, though real-world applications remain speculative. While AI can streamline repetitive tasks, designers warn against overvaluing generative tools, stressing that creativity is inherently human, thoughtful, and nuanced.

text: Detecting and Countering Malicious Uses of Claude. Claude models were misused for influence operations, credential stuffing, recruitment fraud, and malware development. Anthropic is improving safeguards and has banned accounts involved in these activities to prevent further misuse.

title: Norway's Sovereign Wealth Fund Boosts Bitcoin Exposure 83% in Q2
text: Norway's Sovereign Wealth Fund Boosts Bitcoin Exposure 83% in Q2. Norges Bank Investment Management, the world's largest sovereign wealth fund, increased its bitcoin-linked holdings from 6,200 BTC to 11,400 BTC in Q2. The fund's exposure is almost entirely through MicroStrategy, with a small addition in Metaplanet shares. The proactive move is part of a broader trend of sovereign entities gaining indirect BTC exposure.

title: Banks, fintechs halt mortgage lending in California wildfire zones
text: Banks, fintechs halt mortgage lending in California wildfire zones. Some of the country's largest mortgage lenders are pausing originations in parts of California due to rising wildfire risks and escalating insurance costs. Lenders like Rocket Mortgage and Wells Fargo are pulling back from high-risk zones, while fintechs are increasingly using climate data to assess long-term property risk. This shift is leaving homebuyers in fire-prone areas with fewer financing options, underscoring a growing divide between insurable and uninsurable markets in climate-vulnerable regions.

title: Founder Boreout Is a Bigger Problem Than Founder Burnout
text: Founder Boreout Is a Bigger Problem Than Founder Burnout. Founder burnout - pushing through those endless workweeks until you crash - gets all the headlines. However, what about founder boreout? That empty feeling when you're stuck doing boring admin tasks instead of the exciting work that made you start your company. Instead of scrolling Twitter while trudging through your inbox (which just makes it worse), try turning mundane work into games—racing to clear your tasks, creating smarter workflows, or finding clever hacks like Feynman did with his bean-cutting technique. Sometimes the fix for feeling bored isn't stepping back, but leaning in.

title: Coinbase acquiring Circle isn't crazy
text: Coinbase acquiring Circle isn't crazy. Circle trades at a $5B market cap with $155M net income and a similar 32x P/E as Coinbase, but with USDC poised to grow alongside global dollar demand and regulatory clarity via the GENIUS Act, its future earnings potential is much larger. title: Airwallex hits $1B revenue run rate as UK momentum surges
text: Airwallex hits $1B revenue run rate as UK momentum surges. Airwallex has surpassed $1 billion in annualised revenue, doubling its run rate in just over a year and marking one of fintech's fastest growth curves. The UK has emerged as the firm's fastest-growing market, with revenue up 109% year over year and headcount in EMEA rising 35%. The payments and banking platform has relocated its EMEA HQ to Fitzrovia, London, and launched a major “Broken Banking” campaign alongside partnerships with McLaren F1 and Arsenal FC. CEO Jack Zhang said the growth reflects rising adoption of Airwallex's global accounts, payments, and embedded finance tools as businesses look to escape outdated banking systems.

title: NetSuite reveals its ‘Next' move at SuiteWorld
text: NetSuite reveals its ‘Next' move at SuiteWorld. Oracle NetSuite unveiled NetSuite Next, a major update that infuses conversational AI, agentic workflows, and natural-language search directly into its ERP suite. The new “Ask Oracle” assistant lets users query data, generate dashboards, and take actions, while new AI Canvas and SuiteAgent tools enable collaborative problem-solving and autonomous task execution. NetSuite also announced BILL-powered payments, predictive planning tools, and new subscription analytics for SaaS metrics like MRR, ARR, and CAC payback.

title: How to Spot ERR
text: How to Spot ERR. Many SaaS companies think they have stable recurring revenue when they actually have experimental revenue that could vanish overnight. The warning signs are everywhere and mistaking ERR for ARR leads to overhiring, burning cash, and fundraising at valuations you can't grow into.

title: Figma's top VCs are sitting on $24 billion worth of stock after massive IPO pop
text: Figma's top VCs are sitting on $24 billion worth of stock after massive IPO pop. Following its massive IPO debut, Figma's value surged to nearly $68 billion, with initial investors like Index Ventures, Greylock, Kleiner Perkins, and Sequoia holding $24 billion in stock. The IPO raised $1.2 billion, with most proceeds going to existing investors, although a lock-up period restricts major sales until January. This marks a shift for the tech IPO market, previously slow due to inflation and market conditions, with Figma standing out as a significant success story.

title: Ethereum's Next 5 Years as the Risk-Free Asset
text: Ethereum's Next 5 Years as the Risk-Free Asset. Ethereum's future lies in proof aggregation, with L2s like ZKsync defragmenting the network and scaling it to millions of TPS, rendering alternative L1s obsolete. The Ethereum L1 remains the source of incorruptibility and neutrality, expected to reach 10,000+ TPS within three years, but still not enough for global demand. As L2s evolve into cashflow machines replacing traditional payment rails (e.g., credit cards, FX, and remittance apps), their success will flow back to Ethereum, which underpins their security. Investors should own the L2s generating yield to accumulate ETH, the risk-free asset of the EVM economy.

title: The Downside of Secondaries
text: The Downside of Secondaries. Secondary sales give employees and investors liquidity in today's slow IPO market, but they come with a significant hidden cost. These transactions raise your company's 409A valuation by providing evidence of common stock value, which increases the exercise price for all future option grants. One executive found secondaries added $0.50 to his option price, costing him an extra $1M on his 2M share grant. When you want to sell shares, secondaries seem great. When you join a company and get options, they reduce your upside. Most employees never know these transactions happened, but they might be silently erasing millions in potential gains.

title: MFTool
text: MFTool. MFTool is a red team-oriented NTFS parser that bypasses Windows file APIs by directly parsing on-disk Master File Table structures to access locked, deleted, and hidden files. The tool can retrieve sensitive files like SAM, SYSTEM, and pagefile.sys without opening OS-level file handles, effectively circumventing Windows file access controls while requiring administrative privileges. It supports regex-based searches across entire volumes and maintains an encrypted in-memory Master File Table (MFT) cache for stealthy file system operations during red team engagements.

title: From 19k to 4.2M events/sec: story of a SQLite query optimisation
text: From 19k to 4.2M events/sec: story of a SQLite query optimisation. Developers working on the Matrix Rust SDK uncovered severe performance issues in fetching chunk metadata from SQLite, where an inefficient LEFT JOIN caused unnecessary scans and query times exceeding a minute. By progressively optimizing—first with a CASE expression, then restructuring into just two queries with Rust-side aggregation—they improved throughput from ~20K to 4.2M events/sec, achieving a 211× speedup without changing schemas or core data structures.

title: Bitcoin Faces Japan Rate Hike: Debunking The Yen Carry Trade
text: Bitcoin Faces Japan Rate Hike: Debunking The Yen Carry Trade. Fears of a yen carry trade unwind from the Bank of Japan's expected 0.75% rate hike are overblown as the move is already priced into Japanese government bond yields near multi-decade highs, and speculators hold net long yen positions, limiting panic buying potential, contrasting with August 2024's bearish positioning that triggered market chaos. The yield differential between Japan's 0.75% and the US' 3.75% remains wide enough to favor American assets and discourage mass carry trade unwinding, with BOJ staying the most dovish major central bank. The real risk lies in Japanese tightening sustaining elevated US Treasury yields that counter Fed rate cuts, dampening global risk appetite by raising borrowing costs, rather than a sudden yen surge, while Trump's fiscal expansion push could stoke debt fears, lifting bond yields further.

title: UUIDv47
text: UUIDv47. UUIDv47 is a C library that provides a method to store sortable UUIDv7 in databases while exposing a UUIDv4-like façade at the API level by XOR-masking the timestamp with a keyed SipHash stream. The library offers a deterministic, invertible mapping between UUIDv7 and the UUIDv4 façade, guaranteeing compatibility with RFC standards and resistance to key recovery. It includes functions for encoding and decoding the façade, parsing and formatting UUIDs, and setting version/variant bits.

The update also introduces new testing and debugging tools like VS Code integration, concurrent tests, and type testing, as well as better security features such as Bun.secrets for encrypted credential storage and CSRF protection.

title: Is resumable LLM streaming hard? No, it's just annoying
text: Is resumable LLM streaming hard? No, it's just annoying. The state-of-the-art in large language model streaming is surprisingly bad. Both Gemini and Claude require users to refresh the page if their conversation is interrupted mid-stream. This guide shows readers how to implement good, resumable LLM streams. This allows users to refresh tabs mid-stream, switch between chats mid-stream, navigate to other sections of an app without interrupting a stream, and continue streams through momentary internet connection disruptions.

title: Addressing the unauthorized issuance of multiple TLS certificates for 1.1.1.1
text: Addressing the unauthorized issuance of multiple TLS certificates for 1.1.1.1. Unauthorized certificates for Cloudflare's 1.1.1.1 public DNS resolver were issued by Fina CA between February 2024 and August 2025, but have since been revoked.

title: Phishing platforms, infostealers blamed as identity attacks soar
text: Phishing platforms, infostealers blamed as identity attacks soar. Identity-based cyberattacks increased 156% due to advanced phishing kits such as Tycoon 2FA and info-stealing malware. Phishing-as-a-service platforms, which cost $200-$300 monthly, make sophisticated attacks accessible, now comprising 59% of security investigations, often resulting in business email compromise (BEC) and ransomware.

title: Tim Cook and other Apple execs talk iPhone Air's design vision in new WSJ interview
text: Tim Cook and other Apple execs talk iPhone Air's design vision in new WSJ interview. The iPhone Air emphasizes thinness, lightness, and a polished, reflective design aimed at style over utility, contrasting with the sturdier Pro models. Apple executives acknowledged that users will face a tough choice between Air and Pro, hinted at secrecy around future foldables, and highlighted personalization features like the crossbody strap as central to the Air's identity.

title: We Have To Simplify!
text: We Have To Simplify!. When leaders say “we need to simplify,” they rarely mean the same thing.

title: CFPB spurns BNPL rule
text: CFPB spurns BNPL rule. The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau has reversed its earlier push to hold buy now, pay later firms to the same standards as traditional credit card issuers. After announcing plans to cut its own workforce by 90%, the CFPB indicated it would shift enforcement and supervisory responsibilities to state regulators and step back from direct oversight of nonbank lenders. The move reflects a broader retreat from federal involvement in fintech regulation.

title: Instagram Supercharges Creation and Feed Control with new Edits App Features and “Your Algorithm”
text: Instagram Supercharges Creation and Feed Control with new Edits App Features and “Your Algorithm”. Instagram's Edits app now includes pre-built templates, storyboards, advanced text tools, and an iPhone lock screen widget for instant camera access and quick content capture. The platform is introducing "Your Algorithm" in the US, allowing users to view and modify the topics Instagram uses to curate their Reels feed by adding or removing interests. These updates aim to streamline content creation for creators while giving regular users more control over their algorithmic feeds, with similar Explore page features planned.

title: Hacking A Banned Chinese Security Camera
text: Hacking A Banned Chinese Security Camera. A security researcher successfully conducted man-in-the-middle attacks against a banned Chinese security camera, demonstrating that the device fails to verify certificate signing chains properly and transmits unencrypted video data over UDP. While these vulnerabilities are concerning, the real security issue driving the US ban is the camera's automatic firmware update capability, which could allow manufacturers to push malicious software updates in the future. The analysis highlights how even devices with multiple security flaws may pose their most significant risk through remote update mechanisms that users cannot control or audit.

title: Protocol Token Buybacks Hit $40M Weekly
text: Protocol Token Buybacks Hit $40M Weekly. Protocols are increasingly mimicking corporate share repurchase strategies, with nearly $40M in weekly token buybacks last week, led by Hyperliquid ($24M) and Pump.fun ($10M). Buybacks aim to reduce supply and signal long-term confidence, but their effectiveness is mixed—prices may benefit temporarily but often face pressure once programs slow. Sustainability depends on strong, recurring fee revenue, making high-revenue protocols better positioned to maintain consistent buybacks than those reliant on depleting treasuries.

title: Most ITSM leaders worry about AI security — but they're already exploring AI for incident management
text: Most ITSM leaders worry about AI security — but they're already exploring AI for incident management. text: OpenVox InfraTales - macOS Signing and Notarization. title: Taking over 60k spyware user accounts with SQL injection
text: Taking over 60k spyware user accounts with SQL injection. This developer discovered a SQL injection vulnerability in the Catwatchful stalkerware service that allowed him to access its database. He successfully dumped the 'user' table, gaining access to over 60,000 plaintext usernames and passwords.

title: Congress Accidentally Created a Federal Payments Charter with Stablecoins
text: Congress Accidentally Created a Federal Payments Charter with Stablecoins. For decades, fintech companies begged Congress for a federal payments charter. State regulators killed it every time. Last month, Congress accidentally gave them something better. We just lived through the most consequential change to financial services regulation in two decades or more with the passing of the GENIUS Act. The result will be the biggest shift in US domestic payments in a generation. For the first time, we have a third way to regulate payments. The FBO account, the MTL, and now, stablecoins. This parallel financial system exists on top of, and around the existing one. Like the internet does over Telcos.

title: Debian isn't waiting for 2038 to blow up, switches to 64-bit time for everything
text: Debian isn't waiting for 2038 to blow up, switches to 64-bit time for everything. Debian will switch to 64-bit time on 32-bit architectures from Debian 13 "Trixie" to prevent the Y2K38 bug, where 32-bit signed integers overflow on January 19, 2038. This affects 6,429 packages and breaks ABI compatibility, but maintainers have fixed all affected libraries. The legacy i386 port keeps 32-bit time for compatibility with existing x86 binaries.

title: Opsqueue: a lightweight batch processing queue for the heaviest loads
text: Opsqueue: a lightweight batch processing queue for the heaviest loads. Channable has open-sourced Opsqueue, a lightweight Rust-based batch processing queue optimized for high-throughput, large-scale workloads. Designed for the "generate-execute" pattern, it supports billions of operations, lets consumers dynamically prioritize tasks, and emphasizes operational simplicity through a single binary with embedded SQLite and object storage integration.

Despite renewed investor enthusiasm, Wall Street remains cautious, with most analysts rating the stock hold or sell as the company struggles to recover from its 2022 downturn.

title: 74% of New Web Pages Include AI Content
text: 74% of New Web Pages Include AI Content. Ahrefs analyzed 900K new web pages and found that 74% contained AI-generated content. Only 25.8% were purely human-written, while 71.7% had a mix of AI and human input. Among the mixed pages, 25.9% had moderate AI use, 20.5% substantial use, and 15.5% dominant AI presence. The study highlights that AI is now the default tool for content creation, with 87% of surveyed content marketers using it.

title: Large-Scale Attack Targeting Macs via GitHub Pages Impersonating Companies to Attempt to Deliver Stealer Malware
text: Large-Scale Attack Targeting Macs via GitHub Pages Impersonating Companies to Attempt to Deliver Stealer Malware. Attackers created fraudulent GitHub Pages impersonating over 100 companies (including LastPass, 1Password, financial institutions, and tech firms) using SEO to rank high in search results and trick Mac users into downloading fake software installers. The malicious sites redirect victims through multiple layers to ultimately execute a terminal command that downloads Atomic Stealer malware, which harvests credentials and sensitive data from infected systems. The campaign leverages trusted GitHub infrastructure and creates multiple backup repositories under different usernames to evade takedown efforts while targeting a broad range of high-value software applications.

title: Range Rover “recodes DNA” with new wordmark and motif
text: Range Rover “recodes DNA” with new wordmark and motif. This shift reflects broader industry changes driven by the rise of electric vehicles and evolving consumer expectations, though critics warn that many rebrands risk losing brand distinctiveness in the pursuit of minimalism and modernity.

text: How Clarify Turned LinkedIn Into Their #1 Lead Machine. Clarify turned LinkedIn into its top lead source in six months by treating the platform as a standalone channel and posting native content that drives conversation. It built a steady content pipeline using weekly executive interviews and organized posts into clear pillars that mixed personal stories, industry insights, product education, and timely reactions. It expanded reach through creator partnerships and consistent employee advocacy. This helped it outperform what a company page can do alone. It tracked high-performing posts, reused winning angles, and steadily compounded momentum until LinkedIn became its strongest inbound engine.

title: Is there a Half-Life for the Success Rates of AI Agents?
text: Is there a Half-Life for the Success Rates of AI Agents?. AI agent performance can be characterized by a "half-life" related to human task completion time. There is an observed exponential decline in success rates with task length and the model proposed in this article allows for predictions of success rates at different task lengths and improvement over time.

text: Don't ignore the “middle child”. Many B2B companies focus heavily on onboarding and renewals but neglect the long "middle" of the customer lifecycle, where engagement often drops off. This period is overlooked due to unclear metrics, ownership confusion, and the difficulty of creating content for established users. This leads to missed expansion opportunities, declining usage, and last-minute renewal scrambles. To address this, companies should implement milestone-triggered communications, create mid-lifecycle value roadmaps, and develop re-onboarding experiences to keep customers engaged.

text: The Not-So Hidden Inflation Crisis Inside B2B Marketing. text: The Artifacts You Need to Get Hired. A four-artifact system helps creative professionals get hired: a website as a digital calling card, a presentation for interviews, a PDF portfolio for follow-up sharing, and a resume as proof of work. title: AI coding tools are shifting to a surprising place: the terminal
text: AI coding tools are shifting to a surprising place: the terminal. AI coding tools are shifting from code editors to the terminal, as agentic AI grows more capable and developers seek deeper system-level interactions. Tools like Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and CLI Codex reflect this change. Leading companies are quietly prioritizing command-line interfaces. Unlike traditional code assistants focused on fixing code, terminal-based tools address broader tasks like system setup and debugging. With rising skepticism around code editors' actual productivity gains, terminal-first environments like Warp are emerging as a more versatile alternative.

title: US Senate to hold hearing on crypto taxation
text: US Senate to hold hearing on crypto taxation. The Senate Finance Committee has scheduled a hearing on October 1 to review how cryptocurrency should be taxed, reflecting the growing urgency around modernizing the US tax code for digital assets. The session comes as lawmakers debate exemptions for small, routine crypto purchases and broader reforms. Current IRS rules treat all crypto transactions as taxable events, even when no gains or losses occur.

text: BitChat: Jack Dorsey's Offline Payments App. Block CEO Jack Dorsey has quietly launched BitChat, a peer-to-peer chat and payments app designed to work even when users are offline or off-grid. It's currently in beta and aligns with Dorsey's broader push toward decentralized, resilient financial infrastructure.

title: The Definitive Guide To Pricing Structure
text: The Definitive Guide To Pricing Structure. Stop obsessing over whether to charge $119 or $149 and instead focus on your pricing structure. The most successful SaaS companies use a combination of pricing modalities (Flat Fee, License, Usage, or Credit) with carefully chosen metrics that align with customer value. Research shows that 14% of top companies now blend license and usage-based approaches to capture more revenue. This approach solves the fundamental challenge of pricing: charging different customers different amounts based on their unique value perception and ability to pay.

title: Ethereum Foundation Introduces dAI Team
text: Ethereum Foundation Introduces dAI Team. The Ethereum Foundation's dAI Team aims to make Ethereum the preferred settlement and coordination layer for AIs and the machine economy. It is focusing on the AI Economy on Ethereum that provides AI agents and robots payment, coordination, and governance capabilities without intermediaries, and a Decentralized AI Stack ensuring AI's future doesn't depend solely on centralized entities. The team will collaborate closely with Protocol and Ecosystem teams to link protocol improvements with AI builder needs while funding innovative public goods, continuing work on the ERC-8004 standard for AI agent identity verification and trustworthiness proofs. The initiative operates under the principle that Ethereum makes AI more trustworthy while AI makes Ethereum more useful, as intelligent agents require neutral base layers for value and reputation while Ethereum benefits by becoming that infrastructure layer and AI escapes lock-in to centralized platforms.

title: Musk thinks UX and coding are the same, this absurdity leads to chaos
text: Musk thinks UX and coding are the same, this absurdity leads to chaos. Elon Musk's claim that AI has reached PhD-level intelligence but lacks common sense highlights the broader divide between techno-optimists and human-centered perspectives. Technology and society are inseparable hybrids, and rationality cannot exist without empathy. Engineers often operate in a “nature” bubble focused on code and data, while UX practitioners translate human needs into that world. Ignoring social and emotional dimensions risks creating technologies that work perfectly in theory but fail real people, making UX essential as a bridge between human complexity and technological precision.

title: Google fixes bug that could reveal users' private phone numbers
text: Google fixes bug that could reveal users' private phone numbers. A security researcher discovered a Google bug that allowed attackers to reveal any account's private recovery phone number in under 20 minutes without owner notification. The exploit bypassed anti-bot protections through automated brute-force attacks. Google confirmed the fix after the researcher disclosed the vulnerability in April. It paid a $5,000 bounty and found no confirmed exploits occurred.

title: Buy now, pay later becoming economic necessity for merchant POS systems
text: Buy now, pay later becoming economic necessity for merchant POS systems. Shoppers increasingly value speed, trust, and flexibility at checkout. 61% of BNPL users are more likely to shop where BNPL options are available. Meanwhile, 88% of merchants are not currently offering BNPL, and 90% of merchants are not currently offering POS finance but say they would do so if backed by a regulated bank. This highlights a growing appetite for responsible, bank-led solutions that complement the in-store experience.

title: The Radical Simplicity of Massimo Frascella: Inside the Mind of Audi's New Design Chief
text: The Radical Simplicity of Massimo Frascella: Inside the Mind of Audi's New Design Chief. Audi's new Chief Creative Officer, Massimo Frascella, who wasn't originally a car enthusiast, found his design philosophy shaped by the original TT's rational yet emotional approach when he first encountered it at Carrozzeria Bertone in 1998. His "Radical Next" philosophy mandates four principles—clarity, technicality, intelligence, and emotion—that must all be present in every Audi design, from the production-ready Concept C to the newly revealed Formula 1 car. Frascella emphasizes substance over spectacle, utilizing innovative, sustainable materials to redefine luxury while ensuring that concept cars preview actual production models rather than unattainable fantasies.

title: How Cloudflare's client-side security made the npm supply chain attack a non-event
text: How Cloudflare's client-side security made the npm supply chain attack a non-event. Attackers used phishing to compromise npm maintainer accounts in September, injecting malicious code into 18 popular packages with over 2 billion weekly downloads to steal cryptocurrency and credentials from CI/CD pipelines. Cloudflare's Page Shield, using ML-based analysis of 3.5 billion scripts daily, detected all compromised packages with 98% precision and 90% recall by identifying obfuscated code and changes to wallet interfaces. Organizations should audit dependencies, rotate credentials, pin safe versions, and consider Cloudflare's Client-Side Risk Assessment for supply chain security.

title: Institutions Trim BTC While Solana ETFs Keep Absorbing Flows
text: Institutions Trim BTC While Solana ETFs Keep Absorbing Flows. BlackRock's IBIT posted a record $523 million single-day outflow and has now seen $1.43 billion pulled over five days and $2.19 billion over four weeks, as institutions rebalance risk since bitcoin's slide from a $126,080 ATH and ongoing Fed rate and liquidity uncertainty. The selling drove a $372.7 million net outflow across all spot BTC ETFs and similar redemptions from ETH products. Spot Solana ETFs have quietly racked up 16 straight days of inflows totaling $420.4 million, signaling allocators rotating toward higher-yielding altcoin exposure rather than exiting crypto entirely.

title: How to Advertise to Distracted Consumers
text: How to Advertise to Distracted Consumers. In lifelike, multi-tasking environments, distracted consumers actually recalled pop-up ads better. This contradicts the traditional Dual-Task Interference theory. Ad recall was 11.4% higher when all content appeared on a single screen and 29.9% higher when ads were thematically aligned with the main content. Timing also mattered - ads placed during less engaging moments saw a 12.9-16.2% boost in recall. Repeated exposure further increased effectiveness, suggesting that automaticity enables ads to benefit from distraction rather than suffer from it.

title: React Reconciliation: The Hidden Engine Behind Your Components
text: React Reconciliation: The Hidden Engine Behind Your Components. React's reconciliation engine is what updates the DOM by comparing a new element tree to the previous one. It determines component identity usually by element type and position, but the key prop can override default behavior to preserve state across renders. This post goes over common performance tips like state colocation, avoiding inline component definitions, and designing components with clear boundaries.

title: Polygon Targets 5,000 TPS by Year-End
text: Polygon Targets 5,000 TPS by Year-End. Key upgrades include the Bhilai hardfork and Heimdall v2, both live on testnet and slated for mainnet deployment in July. title: Fake AI-generated shops, ads flourish on Facebook
text: Fake AI-generated shops, ads flourish on Facebook. Scammers are exploiting Facebook's ad network using AI to create fake online shops that appear credible. These ads promote trending items with authentic-looking visuals and attractive prices. When clicked, users are sent to fraudulent e-commerce sites and tricked into making purchases. Victims often receive knockoffs or no product at all. One case involved fake ads for UK-based brands C'est La Vie and Mabel & Daisy, which were actually connected to Chinese warehouses. Meta responded by removing the ads, but it continues to profit from scam content. One estimate places annual scam ad revenue at $7 billion.

title: Frequent ReAuth Doesn't Make You More Secure
text: Frequent ReAuth Doesn't Make You More Secure. Low session expiration times frustrate users and don't effectively improve security. Frequent reauthentication aims to prevent credential theft, but if attackers have physical access, a screen lock suffices, and if remote, expiration times are often too long to prevent breaches. Instead, systems should reauthenticate only before sensitive actions and use continuous verification like device checks and SCIM controls.

title: 'I paid for the whole GPU, I am going to use the whole GPU': A high-level guide to GPU utilization
text: 'I paid for the whole GPU, I am going to use the whole GPU': A high-level guide to GPU utilization. GPU utilization has three key metrics: GPU Allocation Utilization (time spent running application code), GPU Kernel Utilization (time spent running code on the GPU), and Model FLOP/s Utilization (fraction of arithmetic bandwidth used). Utilization is defined as output achieved divided by capacity paid for, so optimizing each level is necessary to maximizing the return on investment in GPUs. Common bottlenecks include slow allocation processes, host overhead, memory bandwidth limitations, and poorly optimized kernels.

title: How US Merchants Fail EU Consumers
text: How US Merchants Fail EU Consumers. US merchants often misread Europe as one unified market. However, consumer habits, languages, and trust cues vary widely. Germany favors detailed specs, strict return clarity, and payment after delivery. France prioritizes native language, brand storytelling, and culturally aligned presentation. The Nordics expect seamless mobile commerce and clear sustainability details, while Southern Europe is more price sensitive and focused on reliable delivery. Merchants need to tailor payments, logistics, and branding to each country.

title: Rust running on every GPU

The project uses Rust GPU, Rust CUDA, and Naga to bridge different graphics backends and compile kernels to appropriate device formats.

title: Disrupting the first reported AI-orchestrated cyber espionage campaign
text: Disrupting the first reported AI-orchestrated cyber espionage campaign. A Chinese state-sponsored group manipulated the Claude Code AI tool in mid-September 2025 to infiltrate roughly 30 global targets, marking what is believed to be the first documented case of a large-scale cyberattack executed without substantial human intervention. The AI was used to perform 80-90% of the campaign, analyzing target systems, producing exploit code, and scanning stolen data at speeds impossible for human hackers. It is advised that security teams should experiment with applying AI for defense, as this attack demonstrates how threat actors are adapting their operations to exploit today's most advanced AI capabilities.

title: Ascension reveals personal data of 437,329 patients exposed in cyberattack
text: Ascension reveals personal data of 437,329 patients exposed in cyberattack. Ascension, the second-largest private healthcare system in the US, suffered a data breach that exposed the personal and health information of 437,329 patients through a former partner's compromise. In December 2024, the partner faced a security incident due to a vulnerability in third-party software, leading to the theft of sensitive data, including names, contact details, SSNs, and medical visit records. This marks Ascension's second major cybersecurity incident, following a Black Basta ransomware attack in May 2024, prompting the company to offer affected patients two years of free identity monitoring.
